title: Teaching Fellow in Marketing
description: This Teaching Fellow position in Marketing requires candidates to engage in teaching, learning, and scholarly activities while contributing to programme planning and course development. Applicants must hold a PhD in a relevant discipline, demonstrate strong subject expertise and communication skills, and have a successful track record in teaching or professional sectors such as industry, commerce, or public service. The role will be conferred the title of Lecturer and offers particular value to candidates with substantial experience in curriculum design and student development, though tertiary teaching experience is preferred rather than mandatory. The position comes with a competitive remuneration package on an initial fixed-term contract.

title: Teaching Fellow (Temporary Appointment)
department: Department of Data Science and Artificial Intelligence
description: This is a twelve-month teaching-focused position in Data Science and Artificial Intelligence requiring a PhD in Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or related fields, plus at least three years of tertiary teaching experience or equivalent professional experience. The role encompasses undergraduate and postgraduate instruction, student supervision (seminars, tutorials, projects, and theses), curriculum development, and administrative duties within the department. Candidates must demonstrate strong subject expertise, effective teaching skills, excellent English proficiency, and a genuine commitment to student learning, supported by submission of a curriculum vitae and teaching philosophy statement.

title: Teaching Fellow
description: This twelve-month Teaching Fellow position supports curriculum development, delivery, and administration across undergraduate and taught postgraduate design programmes, including Interior Design, Environment Design, Transitional Environments Design, and Architectural Studies. The appointee will write and revise curriculum documents, teach approximately 24+ credit hours, collaborate with staff to align teaching with industry standards, assess student work, and participate in student recruitment and programme promotion activities. The role requires a master's degree in a related design or architecture field and offers the opportunity to shape emerging design education while contributing to multiple interdisciplinary programmes within the School of Design.

title: Clinical Associate
description: This position is a clinical teaching role for nursing professionals with at least a master's degree and four years of post-registration clinical experience. The appointee will teach pre-registration and postgraduate nursing programmes, supervise students in clinical settings, coordinate clinical placements with healthcare agencies, and contribute to curriculum development and quality improvement initiatives. Candidates must hold current Hong Kong nursing registration and demonstrate a strong track record as accomplished clinical educators with experience in clinical education innovations. The role is distinctive for its emphasis on bridging clinical practice and education while supporting a highly-ranked nursing school committed to developing critical thinkers and ethical healthcare leaders.

title: Chair Professor / Professor in Urban Safety and Health under Strategic Hiring Scheme
description: This position seeks a senior academic leader to establish world-class research and education in urban safety and health within the Faculty's construction and environment disciplines. The appointee will drive strategic planning aligned with institutional goals, build partnerships across academia, industry, and government, and conduct high-impact interdisciplinary research while securing competitive funding. Key responsibilities include mentoring junior faculty, delivering undergraduate and postgraduate teaching, and positioning the field as a leading area within the Faculty's broader mission of advancing sustainable urbanization and smart city development in the region.

title: Research Assistant Professor in Bioinformatics for Multi-omics Studies
department: Department of Applied Biology and Chemical Technology
description: This Research Assistant Professor position focuses on developing and applying computational methods to analyze multi-omics data, including ribosome profiling, mass spectrometry proteomics, NGS, and spatial transcriptomics. The role requires a PhD in bioinformatics, bioengineering, applied mathematics, computer science, or related field, with preferably two years of postdoctoral experience, strong programming skills, and a demonstrated research portfolio in omics research. Primary responsibilities include conducting high-impact research and securing external grants as Principal or Co-Investigator, while contributing to undergraduate and postgraduate teaching and departmental service. The ideal candidate will have solid biological knowledge, ability to communicate with biological and clinical partners, and independent research capability.

title: Research Assistant Professor in Optimization and Operations Research / Statistics and Data Science / Financial Mathematics / Engineering and Computational Mathematics / Mathematical Science
description: The Department of Applied Mathematics seeks a Research Assistant Professor to conduct independent research in optimization, operations research, statistics, data science, financial mathematics, or related computational areas, with expectation to secure external research funding as a principal or co-investigator. The role combines significant research responsibilities with undergraduate and postgraduate teaching duties and departmental service contributions. The successful candidate will engage with the department's joint laboratories with the Chinese Academy of Sciences, CITIC, and Huawei, and will benefit from a large department of 54 academic staff and 105 research personnel offering expertise across optimization, data science, machine learning, and fintech. This position supports the department's expanding portfolio, including new degree schemes launching in 2025/26 and a new AI-focused master's program in 2026/27.

title: Research Assistant Professor in Smart Manufacturing
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This Research Assistant Professor position in Smart Manufacturing requires conducting and publishing high-quality research in top-tier journals while actively pursuing external research funding as Principal Investigator. The role combines research responsibilities with teaching at undergraduate and postgraduate levels and departmental service contributions. Candidates must hold a PhD in a related discipline, preferably with at least two years of postdoctoral experience, along with demonstrated research creativity, effective teaching abilities, and strong English communication skills. The position is embedded within a department offering diverse multi-disciplinary programmes with active applied research and community engagement.

title: Research Assistant Professor in Smart Manufacturing Systems / Physical AI / Embodied Intelligence
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This Research Assistant Professor position requires candidates to conduct high-impact research in smart manufacturing and robotic systems while actively pursuing external research grants as Principal Investigator. Applicants must hold a PhD in Mechanical/Manufacturing Engineering, Robotics, Mechatronics, or related fields, with preferably two years of postdoctoral experience and demonstrated expertise in areas such as robotics AI, sim-to-real transfer, physics-based simulation, robotic mechanisms, or smart manufacturing. The role combines research leadership with teaching responsibilities at undergraduate and postgraduate levels and departmental service contributions. Strong candidates will demonstrate creativity and excellence in research, effective teaching abilities, and excellent communication skills in English.

title: Research Assistant Professor
description: The Department of Computing seeks Research Assistant Professors to lead research programs in four specialized areas: Generative Modeling, Multimodal Learning, Generative AI and Computer Music, and Efficient AI. Appointees will be expected to conduct cutting-edge research while actively securing external grants as principal or co-investigators, teach at undergraduate and postgraduate levels, and supervise research students. The role also involves academic and departmental administrative responsibilities. Candidates should have demonstrated expertise and experience in one of the four specified research domains within computing and artificial intelligence.

title: Research Assistant Professor in AI and Digital Transformation for Education
description: This position is a Research Assistant Professor role focused on advancing artificial intelligence applications in higher education, based in the Department of Computing and working with an institute for higher education research. Key responsibilities include conducting independent and collaborative research on AI in education, designing and implementing digital teaching transformation initiatives, developing training programs for staff and students, creating AI-enabled platforms and policies, and supervising research staff and postgraduate students. The successful candidate must hold a PhD in Computer Science, AI, Educational Technology, or related field with at least two years of postdoctoral research experience, along with a strong publication record and demonstrated research impact. The position is distinctive for its focus on bridging AI research with institutional teaching innovation and capacity building across a university.

title: Research Fellow
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This is a two-month Research Fellow position in the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering focused on supporting a project that develops physics-informed machine learning frameworks for advanced multi-modular 3D bioprinting systems in digital biomanufacturing. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree plus at least three years of postdoctoral research experience, with demonstrated expertise in biomaterials and additive manufacturing. The role involves assisting the project leader with research activities in this emerging intersection of bioengineering, materials science, and computational methods. A competitive remuneration package is offered, with applications closing on 13 March 2026.

title: Project Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This seven-month position supports a flipped learning project in engineering by preparing educational materials, assisting with teaching and learning activities, and conducting educational research tasks. The successful candidate must hold an honours degree and possess strong expertise in engineering management, serious games, and educational research methodologies. Primary responsibilities include developing content for flipped learning in engineering subjects and supporting the project leader with ad-hoc assignments. Full-time positions offer competitive remuneration, while part-time roles are compensated at HK$125 per hour.

title: Research Assistant / Project Assistant
description: This position is a twelve-month Research Assistant role supporting a project to co-design and test a digital sexual health platform for Chinese sexual and gender minority women. The appointee will coordinate project activities including interviews and workshops, prepare academic materials such as literature reviews and data analysis, liaise with NGO and community collaborators, and provide administrative support. Candidates should hold an honours degree in Nursing, Social Sciences, Public Health, Psychology or related fields, with demonstrated research interest in sexual minority and transgender populations, proficiency in research software (SPSS, NVivo, R) and Microsoft Office, and fluency in both English and Chinese. Preference will be given to applicants with prior project coordination, NGO collaboration, or health promotion experience.

title: Research Associate
description: This is a twelve-month Research Associate position in Biomedical Engineering focused on assisting with a project on intelligent pathology using multi-parameter in-vivo optical imaging. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in Optical Engineering with at least three years of research experience, combined with expertise in biomedical optics—particularly fluorescence lifetime imaging or endoscopy. The role requires demonstrated research leadership experience, specifically having served as Principal Investigator or Co-Principal Investigator on at least one national-level research project. The position offers a competitive remuneration package, with applications closing on 12 March 2026.

title: Research Associate
department: Department of Health Technology and Informatics
description: This is a six-month Research Associate position in the Department of Health Technology and Informatics focused on assisting with a project on IT infrastructure, collaborations, and operations support. Candidates must hold a master's degree or a strong honours degree combined with at least three years of relevant research or work experience. The role offers competitive remuneration, with applications closing on 12 March 2026.

title: Project Associate
description: This is a twelve-month administrative support role assisting a project leader focused on enhancing student experience, research impact, and internationalization within the optometry school. Key responsibilities include providing administrative support to academic programmes, coordinating events, compiling reports and maintaining databases, liaising with internal and external stakeholders, and handling general enquiries. The ideal candidate holds a master's degree or good honours degree with three or more years of relevant higher education administration experience, demonstrates fluency in English, Cantonese, and written Chinese, and possesses strong organizational, communication, and interpersonal skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks independently.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering is recruiting full-time or part-time research staff at three levels—Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant—to support a project on glassy-carbon pyrolysis and shaping technologies under the direction of a project leader. Candidates should hold a doctoral degree (Fellow), master's or honours degree (Associate), or honours degree (Assistant), with experience requirements ranging from none to three-plus years depending on position. Preference is given to applicants with expertise in finite element simulations and high-temperature coatings. Appointments are initially for three to twelve months with potential for renewal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: This is a twelve-month postdoctoral position in aeronautical and aviation engineering where the appointee will support the project leader on a research initiative focused on financial support for book writing. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree or equivalent qualification with no more than five years of post-qualification experience. The role offers a competitive remuneration package, and applications are being accepted from March 12, 2026 onwards.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Assistant
description: The Department of Computing seeks one to two Postdoctoral Fellows (12-24 months each) and two to three Research Assistants (6-12 months each) to support a research project on multimodal content generation and editing. Postdoctoral Fellows must hold a PhD in Computer Science, Electrical and Computer Engineering, or related discipline, with no more than five years of post-qualification experience including advanced research in visual computing, deep learning, and/or communication networks. Research Assistants require a BSc in Computer Science, Electrical and Computer Engineering, Mathematics, or related field, with research experience in visual content analytics. Interested candidates should contact Professor Chen Changwen for further details.

title: Part-time Project Technical Assistant
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: This part-time position offers eight months of work assisting a project leader on research into human-centric driver support systems and training for safer cross-border driving by Hong Kong vehicles traveling northbound. The role requires an honours degree or above and pays HK$120 per hour. The position is distinctive in its focus on improving driver safety through technology and training recommendations for a specific cross-border travel context. Applications close on 18 March 2026.

title: Project Technical Assistant
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: This six-month position (with possible extension) in the Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering involves assisting a project leader with research on aerodynamic and noise characteristics of coaxial counter-rotating rotors for advanced unmanned aircraft systems. Candidates must hold a higher diploma or equivalent qualification in an appropriate discipline with at least three years of post-qualification experience, preferably with backgrounds in aerodynamics, aeronautical engineering, or mechanical engineering. The role offers a highly competitive remuneration package and requires applications by 22 July 2026.

title: Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: This is a two to three-month full-time or part-time research assistant position supporting a community-based participatory research project on healthy ageing and working in late life among disadvantaged older adults in Hong Kong. Core responsibilities include conducting and transcribing interviews, assisting with data analysis and dissemination, facilitating training sessions, and supporting overall research activities. Candidates must hold an honours degree in social work, education, or related social sciences disciplines, with demonstrated experience or strong interest in working with older people, social work education, and community development. Fluency in both English and Cantonese Chinese is essential, alongside proficiency in written communication.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Assistant
description: The Department of Applied Social Sciences seeks a Postdoctoral Fellow or Research Assistant for a twelve-month appointment to support longitudinal research projects investigating technostress and well-being among Chinese university students, and adolescent smartphone use within family digital environments. Responsibilities include conducting research and evaluation studies, collecting and analysing data, drafting manuscripts, and supporting administrative tasks. The Postdoctoral Fellow position requires a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, while the Research Assistant position requires an honours degree; both applicants should demonstrate knowledge of well-being and mental health in applied contexts, research experience in youth development or digital tools, and fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua). Essential competencies include strong analytical and statistical skills (SPSS, Mplus, or R), the ability to communicate across technical and non-technical stakeholders, and interdisciplinary collaboration experience.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow (Full-time/Part-time)
description: This postdoctoral position in mechanical engineering involves assisting with research on wearable hydrogel microneedle technology for continuous glucose monitoring, encompassing mechanism studies, prototype development, and in vivo validation. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience and will be appointed for 12-24 months on either a full-time or part-time basis. The role offers competitive compensation for full-time positions or HK$385 per hour for part-time work. Applications are being accepted from 11 March 2026 until the position is filled.

title: Principal Project Fellow
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This is a four-month postdoctoral research position in industrial and systems engineering focused on advanced extrusion processes for manufacturing stiffened panels and curved tubular profiles. The successful candidate will support the project leader in conducting this specialized research. Applicants must hold a doctoral degree with at least eight years of substantial postdoctoral research experience, demonstrating high academic achievement and a strong reputation as a researcher. The position offers competitive remuneration, with applications closing on 17 June 2026.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This two-month research assistant position focuses on developing and validating methods for reliable large language model agents through machine learning and data science research. Key responsibilities include creating a cascading mechanism for efficient model selection, investigating conformal prediction techniques to ensure bounded error rates across tasks, and benchmarking approaches in computational pathology. Candidates must hold an honours degree in a relevant field (such as Safety/Reliability Engineering, Computer Science, or Electrical Engineering) and demonstrate a publication record in reliability engineering and machine learning. The role emphasizes high-quality academic research capability and works directly under a project leader.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This twelve-month postdoctoral position involves assisting a project leader in research and development of key technologies for industrial AI. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, possess demonstrated research experience, and have expertise in AI applications for modelling and optimizing industrial systems. The role requires fluency in English and offers competitive remuneration. The position is distinctive in its specific focus on translating AI methodologies to industrial applications rather than foundational AI research.

title: Research Associate
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This six-month Research Associate position involves assisting with a project focused on converting waste tires into value-added products through novel process design, simulation, and optimization. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree and demonstrate research experience combined with expertise in process optimization, ESG assessment, and multi-criteria decision-making. Strong English language proficiency is essential. A competitive remuneration package is offered for this appointment.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Food Science and Nutrition
description: This twelve-month postdoctoral position involves assisting the project leader with research on sustainable urban green agriculture within the Food Science and Nutrition department. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree or equivalent qualification and have no more than five years of post-qualification experience. The role offers competitive remuneration, with applications reviewed from March 10, 2026 onwards.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Food Science and Nutrition
description: This postdoctoral position is a twelve-month appointment in which the fellow will assist with research on hypervirulent and multidrug-resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae, addressing a significant global public health threat through multidisciplinary approaches. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree or equivalent qualification with no more than five years of post-qualification experience at the time of application. The role offers competitive remuneration, with applications reviewed from March 10, 2026 onwards until positions are filled.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Assistant
description: This position supports research on an empathetic navigation system that infers driver emotions from traffic data to improve route planning. The Postdoctoral Fellow role requires a doctoral degree in Computer Science with up to five years post-qualification experience, expertise in path planning, proficiency with CARLA simulation and mapping APIs, and strong programming skills in Python and Java. The three Research Assistant positions require an honours degree and hands-on experience in experimental design, data collection, and analysis, or strong programming capabilities. All positions are initially four-month appointments with potential extension, focusing on system development and simulation testing.

title: Senior Research Fellow / Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time / Part-time)
description: The Department of Applied Mathematics seeks to fill multiple positions across five levels—from Senior Research Fellow to Research Assistant—on full-time or part-time bases for six to twelve-month appointments. All appointees will support a research project on mechanisms and key technologies for multi-sensory emulation wearable devices under the direction of a project leader. Qualifications range from a doctoral degree with six years of postdoctoral experience for Senior Research Fellow roles down to an honours degree for Research Assistant positions, with intermediate levels requiring progressively less seniority. Interested applicants should contact Prof. Sun Defeng for further details.

title: Senior Research Fellow / Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time / Part-time)
description: Multiple full-time and part-time positions across five levels are available within the Department of Applied Mathematics to support a research center focused on intelligent operations research. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree with varying postdoctoral experience (ranging from none for Postdoctoral Fellows to six+ years for Senior Research Fellows), or equivalent qualifications in research or relevant fields. Research Associates and Research Assistants may hold master's or honours degrees with appropriate professional experience. All appointments are fixed-term contracts lasting between six and twelve months.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: School of Hotel and Tourism Management
description: This nine-month postdoctoral position involves assisting with a research project analyzing competitive strategies between short-term rentals and hotels. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience and demonstrate either strong interest in publishing strategic management research or advanced skills in secondary data analysis. The role suits researchers with quantitative expertise or ambitions to develop high-impact publications in the hospitality and tourism sector. A competitive remuneration package is provided.

title: Research Assistant
department: School of Hotel and Tourism Management
description: This is a six-month Research Assistant position in the School of Hotel and Tourism Management supporting a project to develop a tourism demand forecasting platform for the Greater Bay Area. The role requires an honours degree in Marketing or Digital Marketing, along with expertise in content creation and digital marketing, plus strong English writing skills. The position offers competitive remuneration and applications are being accepted from March 10, 2026 onwards.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
department: Department of Building Environment and Energy Engineering
description: This is a 12-month research position focused on investigating indoor environmental quality and energy consumption in Hong Kong school classrooms and their effects on student health and academic performance. Candidates for the Research Associate role require a doctorate or master's degree in Engineering or Science (or equivalent with 3+ years' experience), while Research Assistant applicants need an honours degree in these fields. Essential qualifications include hands-on experience with IEQ field measurements, energy simulations, and machine learning modelling, alongside strong analytical abilities and independent research capability. Bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (including Cantonese) is required for both positions.

title: Research Assistant
description: This five-month research assistant position involves supporting a project leader in developing textile actuators for sports haptic simulation. Candidates must hold an honours degree in Materials, Mechanical Engineering, Polymers, Textiles, Electrical Engineering, or related field, with strong hands-on expertise spanning polymer processing, microfabrication, mechanical design, textile manufacturing, electrical circuits, and 3D printing. The role requires excellent communication skills and demonstrated motivation to achieve research objectives. The position offers competitive remuneration and applications are reviewed starting 27 March 2026.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
department: Department of Health Technology and Informatics
description: This 6-7 month position offers two appointment levels for researchers to support a clinical trial investigating non-invasive brain stimulation and perceptual learning as treatments to improve vision and quality of life in glaucoma patients. Postdoctoral Fellows must hold a doctorate with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, while Research Associates require a master's degree or strong honours degree plus at least three years of relevant research experience. Candidates must demonstrate strong wet-lab competencies including molecular biology techniques, cell culture, genotyping, and proficiency with unlabelled probe melting analysis. The role involves assisting the project leader with research implementation and execution.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: This twelve-month postdoctoral position involves assisting with a research project focused on developing 3D mapping-aided GNSS PPP-RTK technology. Applicants must hold a doctoral degree or equivalent qualification and have no more than five years of post-qualification experience. The role offers a competitive remuneration package, with applications being accepted from 9 March 2026 onwards until the position is filled.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: This twelve-month postdoctoral position involves assisting the project leader in developing multifunctional aerogel-based interleaves for aerospace-grade carbon fibre reinforced polymer composites, focusing on design and underlying mechanisms. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in Materials Engineering, Materials Science, or a related quantitative discipline, with no more than five years of post-qualification experience. The role offers a highly competitive remuneration package and applications close on 20 July 2026.

title: Project Associate
department: Jockey Club Design Institute for Social Innovation
description: This twelve-month position supports an interdisciplinary project developing expressive arts-based mental health curricula for schools, funded by the Qualitative Education Fund. The Project Associate will lead project delivery, oversee creation of an educational game kit, facilitate Design Thinking workshops with students and teachers, and manage community outreach initiatives. Candidates require a master's degree in Design (preferably Product Design) or an honours degree with three years of relevant experience, plus demonstrated expertise in facilitating co-creation and Design Thinking workshops. The role emphasizes applied social innovation through creative methodologies, requiring occasional work outside standard hours.

title: Senior Research Fellow / Senior Project Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Project Associate / Research Assistant / Project Assistant / Research Technical Assistant / Project Technical Assistant / Research Administrative Assistant / Project Administrative Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: The Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering is recruiting for multiple positions across a tiered structure—from Senior Research Fellow down to Administrative Assistant—to support a research project focused on AI, robotics, and photonics sensing. Positions are available on full-time or part-time bases for twelve-month appointment periods. Qualifications range from a doctoral degree with six years of postdoctoral experience for senior roles, down to secondary education with relevant passes for administrative positions, with intermediate levels requiring master's degrees, honours degrees, or diplomas depending on the specific role. All appointees will work under a project leader to assist with research activities within their respective competency levels.

title: Research Assistant
description: This twelve-month research assistant position supports a project investigating how co-governance structures in family businesses influence the adoption and implementation of artificial intelligence technology. The role requires an honours degree or equivalent qualification and involves assisting the project leader with research activities. The position offers a competitive salary package, with applications due by 31 March 2026.

title: Research Assistant
description: This is a twelve-month research assistant position in the Department of Management and Marketing focused on a project examining opinion polarization modeling and intervention strategies through generative agent interactions. The role involves supporting the project leader in research activities, requiring candidates to hold an honours degree or equivalent qualification. The position offers a competitive remuneration package, with applications closing on 31 March 2026.

title: Research Assistant
description: This is a twelve-month research assistant position in the Department of Management and Marketing supporting a data-driven project focused on preference learning for multi-criteria sorting under uncertainty. The role requires assisting the project leader with research activities on this specialized analytical project. Applicants must hold an honours degree or equivalent qualification. The position offers competitive remuneration, with applications closing on 31 March 2026.

title: Research Assistant
description: This twelve-month Research Assistant position in Management and Marketing involves supporting a project leader on research into collaborative delivery routing using unmanned surface and aerial vehicles for moving targets, employing branch-price-and-cut algorithms and line-of-sight guidance strategies. Applicants must hold an honours degree or equivalent qualification. The role offers competitive remuneration, with applications closing on 31 March 2026.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow
description: These postdoctoral positions involve assisting the project leader in developing an advanced microscopy platform for neural science research. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in Optics, Optical Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related field, with demonstrated expertise in optical instrumentation, light microscopy, and optical imaging systems for biomedical applications. The Research Fellow position requires at least three years of postdoctoral experience, while the Postdoctoral Fellow position is limited to candidates with no more than five years of post-qualification experience. Both positions are initially appointed for twelve months with potential for renewal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This twelve-month postdoctoral position supports research in a joint centre for fiber innovations and renewable materials, with primary responsibilities focused on conducting sustainability research in fashion business and assisting the project leader. Applicants must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, with preference given to candidates with empirical research experience and strong expertise in programming, modeling, and simulations within business contexts. The role offers a competitive remuneration package and welcomes inquiries from prospective candidates.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Assistant
department: Department of Applied Biology and Chemical Technology
description: Two twelve-month positions are available: a Postdoctoral Fellow (requiring a doctoral degree and no more than five years post-qualification experience) and a Research Assistant (requiring an honours degree). Both roles involve assisting the project leader in developing atomically precise catalysts for energy-conversion reactions. A competitive remuneration package is offered, with applications considered from March 5, 2026 onwards.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: The Department of Applied Physics is recruiting for two research positions to support a project on developing hydrogel nanocomposite wound dressing to treat multidrug-resistant bacterial infections using sonodynamic therapy. The Postdoctoral Fellow position (14 months) requires a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, while the Research Associate position (10 months) requires a master's degree or good honours degree with at least three years of relevant research experience. Both roles involve assisting the project leader with research activities and come with a competitive remuneration package. Interested candidates should contact the project leader for additional information.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This position offers full-time or part-time roles (Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, or Research Assistant) within the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering for a twelve-month appointment. Appointees will support research into developing a vision-language model-based smart driving assistant aimed at improving motorist safety and convenience. Qualifications range from a doctoral degree with up to five years of post-qualification experience (Postdoctoral Fellow) to a master's or honours degree with research experience (Research Associate) or an honours degree (Research Assistant). Competitive remuneration is offered for full-time positions, with part-time roles paid at an hourly rate.

title: Senior Project Fellow / Research Associate / Project Associate / Research Assistant / Project Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Computing is recruiting for multiple short-term positions (one to four months) across three levels to support a research project focused on metadata-private and accountable networks at scale. Senior Project Fellow positions require a doctoral degree plus at least six years of postdoctoral experience, while Research Associate/Project Associate roles require a master's degree or strong honours degree with three or more years of research experience, and Research Assistant/Project Assistant positions require an honours degree or equivalent. All positions are available on full-time or part-time bases, with appointees assisting the project leader with research activities. Interested applicants should contact Prof. Allen M. H. Au for further details.

title: Senior Research Fellow / Senior Project Fellow / Research Fellow / Project Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Project Associate / Research Assistant / Project Assistant / Research Administrative Assistant / Project Administrative Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Computing seeks multiple research and administrative staff at various levels to support a project on decentralized federated machine learning at scale, conducting analysis, design, and evaluation work. Positions range from Senior Research Fellow through Research Administrative Assistant on full-time or part-time bases, each with appointment periods of one to twelve months. Candidates must possess appropriate qualifications, ranging from doctoral degrees with substantial postdoctoral experience for senior roles to honours degrees for assistant positions, with experience requirements scaling accordingly by level.

title: Senior Research Fellow / Senior Project Fellow / Research Fellow / Project Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Project Associate / Research Assistant / Project Assistant / Research Administrative Assistant / Project Administrative Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Computing is recruiting for multiple positions across six tiers—from research administrative assistants to senior research fellows—to support a project on threshold signatures for blockchain-based cryptocurrency and anonymous credentials. Positions are available on either full-time or part-time bases with flexible appointment periods of one to twelve months. Qualifications range from an honours degree for entry-level research assistants to a doctoral degree plus at least six years of postdoctoral experience for senior fellows, with intermediate levels requiring increasingly substantial research credentials and experience. All appointees will work under a project leader on the research initiative focused on advanced cryptographic applications in blockchain technology.

title: Senior Research Fellow / Senior Project Fellow / Research Fellow / Project Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Project Associate / Research Assistant / Project Assistant / Research Administrative Assistant / Project Administrative Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Computing is seeking multiple positions at various levels (from administrative assistant to senior research fellow) to support a research project on trustless cross-chain bridges using zero-knowledge proof-of-burn technology. Positions are available on a full-time or part-time basis, with flexible appointment periods of one to twelve months. Minimum qualifications range from an honours degree for assistant roles to a doctoral degree plus six years of postdoctoral experience for senior positions, depending on the specific level. Appointees will assist the project leader with research activities, with the opportunity to work on an emerging blockchain technology area.

title: Senior Research Fellow / Senior Project Fellow / Research Fellow / Project Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Project Associate / Research Assistant / Project Assistant / Research Administrative Assistant / Project Administrative Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Computing seeks to fill multiple positions at various levels (from research administrative assistant to senior research fellow) to support a research project focused on large language model-based vulnerability detection for smart contracts. Positions are available on a full-time or part-time basis with flexible appointment periods of one to twelve months. Requirements range from an honours degree for assistant roles to a doctoral degree plus six years of postdoctoral experience for senior positions, with intermediate levels specifying corresponding qualifications and research experience. Appointees will work under a project leader to assist with research activities related to advanced vulnerability detection technologies in blockchain systems.

title: Senior Research Fellow / Senior Project Fellow / Research Fellow / Project Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Project Associate / Research Assistant / Project Assistant / Research Administrative Assistant / Project Administrative Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Computing seeks multiple staff members at various levels to support a research project focused on advancing autonomous vehicle safety through enhanced automated driving system testing and security assessment. Available positions range from Senior Research Fellows (requiring a doctorate plus 6+ years of postdoctoral experience) down to Research Assistants (requiring an honours degree), with flexibility for both full-time and part-time appointments typically lasting 1-12 months. All appointees will work under the direction of a project leader, with roles tailored to each applicant's qualifications and experience level. The positions offer opportunity to contribute to critical safety research in autonomous vehicle technology.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in the School of Accounting and Finance involves supporting a project investigating differences between earnings used for compensation purposes versus earnings used for valuation. Candidates must hold an honours degree and demonstrate proficiency in statistical programming (Stata or SAS), along with prior research experience in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related quantitative fields. The role is initially offered for three to twelve months with potential for extension, and applicants from leading universities in the US, Australia, New Zealand, Europe, and China are particularly encouraged, though strong candidates from other backgrounds will be considered.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: This position offers a one-year appointment as a Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, or Research Assistant within an aviation research centre, supporting a collaborative project with Boeing on digital aeroengine records. The role involves developing machine learning algorithms for aircraft component inspection, creating automated video-screening systems, designing robot and UAV control systems, and analysing experimental data to validate algorithms for real-time aviation applications. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree (Postdoctoral Fellow), master's degree or strong honours degree with three years' experience (Research Associate), or honours degree with preferred research experience (Research Assistant), and will work across fabrication facilities and laboratories while producing technical documentation.

title: Part-time Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Language Science and Technology
description: This part-time twelve-month postdoctoral position supports a research project investigating ChatGPT's use in personalized writing programs to improve undergraduate students' writing growth mindset and motivation. The fellow will assist the project leader across all research phases, including literature reviews, developing writing tasks and questionnaires, data coding and analysis, project management, and preparing reports and academic papers. Candidates must hold a PhD in Language Education, Educational Psychology, or a related field with no more than five years of post-doctoral experience, along with demonstrated expertise in academic writing, data analysis, and strong English communication skills. The role requires independent work capability and reliability in meeting project deadlines.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Logistics and Maritime Studies
description: This is a three-month research assistant position supporting a project on fee design for platform-based fulfillment services. The role requires an honours degree in Logistics Management, Mathematics, or a related field, alongside technical proficiency in Python, Matlab, data analysis, and Microsoft Office. Candidates should have a publication record in logistics or operational research and demonstrate excellent written and spoken English. A competitive salary package is offered, with applications due by 12 March 2026.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: This is an eight-month postdoctoral position in aeronautical and aviation engineering focused on assisting with research into human-centric driver support systems and safety training for cross-border driving. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree or equivalent qualification with no more than five years of post-qualification experience. The role offers competitive remuneration and applications close on 25 March 2026.

title: Research Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: This position offers full-time or part-time research roles (Fellow, Associate, or Assistant level) within the School of Design for a two-month appointment focused on a project examining movement patterns of older adults using metaverse technology. Responsibilities include assisting with data analysis, academic publication, information collection, report preparation, and other project-related tasks as assigned. Applicants must hold a doctoral degree (Fellow), master's degree (Associate), or honours degree (Assistant) in Design or related fields, with progressively less postdoctoral experience required at lower levels, and must demonstrate expertise in elderly research, academic writing, data analysis, English proficiency, and independent work capabilities.

title: Research Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: This five-month full-time or part-time position supports a research project on robot-assisted object retrieval for individuals with disabilities, requiring appointees to assist with data analysis, publication, information collection, report preparation, and other assigned duties. Candidates for the Research Fellow role need a doctoral degree in Design or related fields plus three years of postdoctoral experience, while Research Associates require a master's degree or strong honours degree with three years of research experience, and Research Assistants need an honours degree in relevant disciplines. All applicants must have experience researching elderly populations, knowledge of academic writing, strong data analysis skills, excellent spoken English, and the ability to work independently within project timelines.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Assistant
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
description: The Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering is recruiting a Postdoctoral Fellow and a Research Assistant (both 12-month contracts) to support a research project investigating bacterial colonisation in the upper respiratory tracts of wastewater treatment plant workers. The Postdoctoral Fellow position requires a doctoral degree in Environmental Microbiology or related field with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, while the Research Assistant position requires an honours degree in Environmental Science, Engineering, or Biology. Both appointees will assist the project leader with research duties related to occupational health and environmental microbiology. A competitive remuneration package is offered.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
description: This four-month Research Assistant position involves supporting a project investigating how antibiotic resistance genes spread through the air under particulate matter pollution, examining the roles of chemical components and emission sources. Candidates must hold an honours degree or equivalent in Engineering, Biology, or Chemistry. The role offers competitive compensation, and interested applicants should contact the project leader directly for additional information.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
description: This is a three-month part-time research assistant position in the Department of Rehabilitation Sciences supporting a project investigating how chronic low back pain affects balance control in older adults. Candidates must hold an honours degree or equivalent qualification. The role involves assisting the project leader with research duties and offers an hourly rate of HK$125, with applications being reviewed from March 4, 2026 onwards.

title: Research Assistant
description: This six-month research assistant position supports a project investigating the use of WhatsApp-based AI chatbots combined with face-to-face physiotherapy to treat chronic low back pain. Candidates must hold an honours degree in Health Sciences, Psychology, Physiotherapy, Nursing, Public Health or related field, with demonstrated experience in clinical research, mHealth, digital health or chronic pain management. Essential requirements include fluency in English and Cantonese Chinese, familiarity with digital platforms like WhatsApp and survey tools, and strong interpersonal and organisational skills. The role demands the ability to collaborate effectively with research participants and multidisciplinary teams in a clinical research environment.

title: Project Associate
description: This twelve-month Project Associate role supports curriculum enhancement for an optometry degree programme, requiring assistance with programme redesign, comparative research on similar offerings at other institutions, documentation preparation, stakeholder communication, and project timeline management. Candidates must hold a master's degree or strong honours degree with three years of relevant experience, preferably in higher education programme administration. The position demands bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (with Cantonese fluency), strong organisational and communication skills, and the ability to manage multiple tasks independently. Ideal applicants will demonstrate responsibility, teamwork orientation, and a commitment to continuous learning.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: The department seeks multiple postdoctoral fellows, research associates, and research assistants for a twelve-month project developing fault self-recovery axial direct-drive hub motors for automated guided vehicles. The postdoctoral fellow positions require a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, while research associates need a master's degree or strong honours degree with three or more years of relevant experience, and research assistants require an honours degree or equivalent. All candidates must have solid expertise in electric machines, finite element methods, and electromagnetic field theory, with strong project completion skills and minimal need for supervision; preference is given to those with experience in machine design and electromagnetic field computation software development.

title: Project Assistant
description: This twelve-month Project Assistant position supports a National Education initiative by coordinating programme planning, implementation, and documentation including data collection and report writing. The role requires administrative support, stakeholder liaison, and record-keeping for project materials and presentations. Candidates must hold an honours degree, demonstrate fluency in English and Chinese, and possess strong organizational and interpersonal skills alongside the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. The position offers the opportunity to contribute to a significant national education project while developing experience in programme coordination and project management.

title: Part-time Project Associate / Project Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This part-time position offers a six to twelve-month initial appointment assisting a project leader on an international professional society initiative. Key responsibilities include coordinating and executing events such as competitions, industrial visits, and seminars; handling administrative tasks including meeting minutes and documentation; and managing social media platforms with content curation and community engagement. Applicants for the Project Associate role require a Master's degree or strong honours degree with three or more years of relevant work experience, while Project Assistant applicants need an honours degree or equivalent qualification.

title: Research Assistant / Project Assistant (Full-time / Part-time)
description: These are full-time or part-time positions lasting three to twelve months supporting a research project investigating how musical training can enhance neuroplasticity and social skills in preschool children with special education needs from disadvantaged backgrounds. Appointees will coordinate and participate in all project phases including literature reviews, participant recruitment, data collection using both quantitative and qualitative methods, data analysis, and administrative support. Successful candidates should hold a relevant honours degree in Nursing, Psychology, Social Sciences or related fields, demonstrate strong research interest in working with children and SEN populations, possess proficiency in Microsoft Excel, Endnote, and preferably statistical software like SPSS or NVivo, and demonstrate excellent English and Chinese language skills alongside strong organizational abilities. A music education background is preferred, and candidates must be able to work independently while maintaining team collaboration and willingness to work off-campus or outside standard office hours.

title: Project Associate / Project Assistant
description: The position is a fixed twelve-month role supporting research operations for a primary health care centre. Responsibilities include coordinating daily centre operations, liaising with NGOs and community partners to promote services, planning and delivering health promotion activities, preparing reports and promotional materials, and performing other assigned duties. Candidates should hold either a master's degree in Health Sciences, Social Sciences, Public Health, Business Administration or related fields, or a strong honours degree with at least three years of relevant work experience (Associate level); or an honours degree for Assistant level. Essential requirements include fluent English and Chinese communication, strong organizational and interpersonal skills, ability to work independently and collaboratively in diverse settings, and demonstrated interest in primary health care and community engagement, with preference given to those with project coordination or NGO experience.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This is a 24-month postdoctoral position in the School of Fashion and Textiles focused on assisting with research into fiber-based technologies enabled by MXenes. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in Materials Science, Engineering, Chemistry, or a related field, with no more than five years of post-qualification experience. Preference is given to those with expertise in MXene synthesis, fiber spinning, advanced characterization, liquid crystals, and wearable technologies. The position offers competitive remuneration and applications are being considered from 2 March 2026 onwards.

title: Research Assistant
department: Division of Artificial Intelligence and the Humanities
description: This is a twelve-month Research Assistant position supporting an artificial intelligence healthcare research project. The role involves conducting literature reviews, data analysis, programming, equipment maintenance, participant recruitment, and data collection for studies focused on multimedia and language learning. Candidates must hold an honours degree and demonstrate strong experience or background in language, technology, neural and computational methodologies or related fields. The position requires collaboration with other project members and flexibility to undertake additional duties as assigned by the project leader.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Project Assistant
department: Research Institute for Artificial Intelligence of Things
description: This posting offers three distinct roles—one Research Fellow, two Postdoctoral Fellows, and one Project Assistant—each on twelve-month contracts to support a multi-year Artificial Intelligence of Things research initiative. The Research Fellow position requires a PhD and minimum three years of postdoctoral AIoT experience, while Postdoctoral Fellow applicants need a doctoral degree in an AIoT-related field with no more than five years of post-qualification experience. The Project Assistant role requires an honors degree in business, public administration, marketing, or event management with one year of relevant work experience and fluency in English, Cantonese, and Putonghua. All appointees will work under a project leader on the institute's 2024-2027 research program.

title: Part-time Research Fellow
department: State Key Laboratory of Ultra-Precision Machining Technology
description: This part-time position involves assisting a project leader in 4D printing research focused on developing reconfigurable micro-/nano-optical devices for dynamic light-field modulation. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in ultra-precision machining technology and precision metrology, with at least three years of postdoctoral research experience or equivalent qualifications. The appointment is for twelve months at HK$394 per hour, with applications reviewed from 27 February 2026 onwards until the position is filled.

title: Senior Research Fellow
description: This is a nine-month Senior Research Fellow position in Applied Mathematics focused on assisting with a research project in data-driven decision making using parametric distributionally robust optimization. The role requires a doctoral degree and at least six years of postdoctoral research experience or equivalent qualifications. The position offers competitive remuneration, with applications being reviewed from 26 February 2026 onwards. Interested candidates should contact the project leader for further details.

title: Research Associate / Project Associate / Research Assistant / Project Assistant (Full-time/Half-time/Part-time)
description: The position supports research on robot-assisted cognitive training, with responsibilities including literature reviews, data collection coordination, preliminary analysis, and recruitment activities. Candidates must hold a master's degree in health-related fields (or an honours degree with three years' experience for associate roles, or an honours degree for assistant roles), with essential requirements including project coordination experience, proficiency in SPSS and Microsoft Office, and bilingual competency in English and Chinese including Putonghua. The role operates on flexible schedules including evenings and weekends, with an initial twelve-month contract and potential extension.

title: Project Technical Assistant / Project Administrative Assistant (Full-time/Half-time/Part-time)
description: This role supports a research project investigating robot-assisted cognitive training for home use, with responsibilities including coordinating logistics across partner institutions, organizing and supporting training activities (dancing, exercise, cognitive work), recruiting participants via social media, liaising with collaborators, and assisting with data entry. The Technical Assistant position requires a higher diploma or equivalent in a health-related field with at least three years' post-qualification experience, while the Administrative Assistant position requires five HKCEE passes (including English, Chinese, and Mathematics) or equivalent HKDSE results. The appointment is initially for six months with possibility of extension, offered on full-time, half-time, or part-time basis, and may require irregular hours including evenings and weekends.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: This is a twelve-month postdoctoral position in electrical and electronic engineering focused on supporting research into sensing technologies for 6G cellular networks. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree and have no more than five years of post-qualification experience. The role involves assisting the project leader with research activities, and the position offers competitive compensation. Applications close on 12 March 2026.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: This twelve-month postdoctoral position involves assisting with a research project combining artificial intelligence with physics-based approaches to enhance reliability validation in power electronics. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience and demonstrate expertise in both power electronics reliability and artificial intelligence. The role offers a competitive remuneration package and applications are being reviewed from mid-March 2026 onwards until the position is filled.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering seeks to fill multiple research positions—two Postdoctoral Fellow roles and Research Associate/Assistant positions—for a 24-month project investigating advanced extrusion processes for stiffened wide-thin panels and curved tubular profiles. Postdoctoral Fellow applicants must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, while Research Associate candidates require a Master's degree or good honours degree with at least three years of relevant research experience, and Research Assistant positions require an honours degree. All appointees will support the project leader's research efforts on this materials processing initiative.

title: Part-time Research Administrative Assistant
description: This is a four-month part-time position in the Department of Computing providing research administrative support for a project developing an LLM-based multi-agent language learning platform with personalized tutoring and contextual simulation capabilities. Applicants must hold five passes in secondary qualifications (either HKCEE or HKDSE), including English, Chinese, and Mathematics at specified levels. The role offers HK$100 per hour compensation, with applications due by 13 March 2026.

title: Project Assistant
description: This is a six-month project assistant position in the Department of Rehabilitation Sciences supporting research on the effectiveness and cost-effectiveness of PelviSense, a wearable biofeedback device for treating stress urinary incontinence in women, as well as evaluating repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation therapies. The role requires an honours degree or above in Physiotherapy or equivalent qualification, along with excellent written and spoken English and Chinese proficiency including fluency in Cantonese. The appointee will provide direct research support to the project leader on a randomised controlled trial with economic evaluation components. A competitive remuneration package is offered, with application review beginning 20 February 2026.

title: Part-time Principal Research Fellow / Part-time Research Assistant
description: The Department of Rehabilitation Sciences seeks two part-time positions for six months to support a research project on home hazard detection and alteration. The Principal Research Fellow role requires a PhD in a relevant discipline (Occupational Therapy, Public Health, Gerontology, or Rehabilitation Sciences) with at least eight years of postdoctoral research experience, ideally in aging, fall prevention, or home safety, plus demonstrated expertise in leading research projects and conducting home assessments for older adults. The Research Assistant position requires an honours degree in a related field such as Occupational Therapy, Public Health, or Nursing. Both positions require fluency in Cantonese and involve assisting the project leader with research activities.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Language Science and Technology
description: This three-month research assistant position supports a neurolinguistic study investigating predictive processing in aging through Large Language Models and neuroimaging data. The role involves working with LLMs in Cantonese and Mandarin Chinese to extract linguistic measures such as surprisal and entropy, requiring strong Python programming skills and familiarity with natural language processing libraries. Candidates must hold an honours degree, demonstrate fluency in English and Putonghua Chinese (with Cantonese as an advantage), and possess excellent teamwork abilities, with prior experience using LLMs for linguistic research being desirable.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Logistics and Maritime Studies
description: This is a three-month research assistant position supporting a project on strategic shipping investment decisions related to emerging green technologies. The role requires assisting the project leader with research activities and candidates must hold an honours degree or equivalent qualification. The position offers competitive remuneration, with applications closing on 8 May 2026.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: This postdoctoral position in electrical and electronic engineering involves assisting the project leader on a research initiative developing a universal time-series foundation model that integrates power electronics reliability monitoring with grid-edge sensing applications. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience and demonstrate expertise in both power electronics reliability and artificial intelligence. The appointment is for twelve months with a competitive remuneration package, with applications reviewed from mid-March 2026 onwards.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
description: This part-time research assistant position in biomedical engineering offers a two-month appointment to support a clinical research project on ultrasound-guided muscle training for stroke patients. Responsibilities include refining study protocols, conducting clinical trials, analyzing data, interpreting findings, and preparing publications. Candidates must hold an honours degree or master's degree in Rehabilitation, Healthcare, or Biomechanics, with demonstrated experience in rehabilitation training, human motion analysis research, health assessment, and data analysis skills. Fluency in spoken English and Chinese (including Putonghua) and strong communication abilities for participant engagement are essential requirements.

title: Project Associate / Part-time Project Associate / Part-time Project Assistant
description: The Department of Applied Social Sciences is recruiting for multiple positions (full-time and part-time) to support a seven-month project on understanding learning difficulties, with responsibilities including coordinating and assisting in service-learning activities for students, providing logistical support, and handling research and administrative tasks. Project Associates require a master's degree in Psychology, Education, Counselling, or related social sciences (or a good honours degree with three+ years of relevant experience), while Project Assistants need an honours degree in these disciplines. All candidates must have experience working with children and youth with special educational needs, fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese, proficiency with online teaching applications, and strong interpersonal and organisational skills.

title: Project Assistant
description: This ten-month position supports a project focused on service leadership for children and families with special needs, based in Chengdu, requiring assistance with student service coordination, research and data analysis, report writing, and project administration. Candidates must hold an honours degree in Psychology, Social Work, Education, or Public Health, with fluent English and Chinese proficiency and proficiency in standard Microsoft Office applications. The role demands strong interpersonal and organizational skills, independence, and teamwork capability, with occasional availability for evening, weekend, and holiday work. This is a support-focused position ideal for someone seeking research and fieldwork experience in social services with a community-based component.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
description: This seven-month part-time position supports a research project investigating cultural sensitivity development through metaverse technology in educational settings. Responsibilities include project administration, participant recruitment and data collection coordination, conducting evaluative research, performing statistical data analysis, and drafting project reports and papers. Candidates should hold an honours degree in Computer Science, Psychology, Education or related fields, with proficiency in statistical software (SPSS, Mplus, R) and bilingual competency in English and Chinese. Prior research experience is preferred, and appointees must demonstrate strong interpersonal and organisational skills, ability to work both independently and collaboratively, and flexibility to work irregular hours including evenings and weekends.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Project Associate / Research Assistant / Project Assistant (Full-time/Half-time/Part-time)
description: This position offers multiple levels of research support (postdoctoral fellow through project assistant) within a 12-24 month appointment to assist with a practice research project on advancing social work through demonstration sites in mainland China. Candidates must hold relevant qualifications ranging from a doctorate (postdoctoral) to an honours degree (assistant level) in social work, social policy, or sociology, with corresponding years of experience. Essential requirements include fluency in both English and Mandarin Chinese, strong social work background, excellent interpersonal and organizational skills, and occasional travel to mainland China. Preference is given to applicants with prior work experience in mainland China, Hong Kong, or public organizations.

title: Project Associate
description: This nine-month Project Associate position supports research on capital allocation practices in internet and artificial intelligence firms. The role involves conducting literature reviews, designing research instruments, collecting financial data, and performing statistical analysis on large corporate datasets, alongside liaison with project collaborators. Candidates must hold a master's degree in business, finance, sociology, or related social sciences (or equivalent with three years' experience), demonstrate proficiency in English and Chinese including Cantonese, and possess strong statistical skills in SPSS and R. The ideal candidate will be self-motivated, well-organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple tasks effectively.

title: Research Assistant
description: This ten-month research assistant position supports a project investigating healthcare access barriers for foreign domestic workers with non-communicable diseases. Key responsibilities include conducting literature reviews, designing research instruments, collecting and analyzing data using both qualitative and quantitative methods, and liaising with collaborators. Applicants must hold an honours degree in Social Sciences or related field, demonstrate fluency in English, and possess strong statistical skills with proficiency in NVivo, SPSS, and R software. The role requires flexibility to work irregular hours including weekends, with preference given to candidates holding a master's or PhD degree and prior research experience with migrant populations or ethnic minorities.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position in Mechanical Engineering involves assisting the project leader on research developing generative AI algorithms for haptic rendering in human-computer and human-robot interactions. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree and have no more than five years of post-doctoral experience. The appointment is for twelve months with a competitive salary package, and the application deadline is 9 April 2026.

title: Research Administrative Assistant
description: This is a twelve-month research administrative assistant position in the Department of Computing supporting a project on privacy-preserving AI agents. The appointee will provide administrative assistance to the project leader for day-to-day research operations. Applicants must hold five passes in secondary education examinations including English, Chinese, and Mathematics, meeting either HKCEE or HKDSE qualification standards. The position offers competitive remuneration, with applications accepted from February 20, 2026 onwards.

title: Assistant Event Organiser
description: This twelve-month position supports the Educational Development Centre's event planning and management operations as part of a dissemination project, assisting the project leader with event logistics, vendor coordination, promotional materials, and on-site execution. The role requires either a master's degree or honours degree with three or more years of relevant experience (depending on whether hired at Project Associate or Project Assistant level), along with demonstrated event management experience in higher education settings. Essential competencies include strong English communication skills, proficiency with Microsoft Office and generative AI tools, exceptional organisational abilities to manage multiple tasks simultaneously, and the capacity to liaise effectively with academic units, vendors, and diverse stakeholders. The successful candidate will be detail-oriented, proactive, and capable of handling everything from event planning and registration coordination to post-event follow-up in a fast-paced environment.

title: Research Technical Assistant
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: This five-month position involves assisting the project leader on research into deploying drones for rapid delivery of Automated External Defibrillators in cardiac arrest response. Candidates must hold an honours degree in Transportation, Management Science, or a related discipline. The role offers competitive remuneration and applications are being reviewed from 20 February 2026 onwards until the position is filled.

title: Research Technical Assistant
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: This is a one-month Research Technical Assistant position in the Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering supporting a project on integrated air cargo service network planning.
