Applicants must hold a PhD in Sociology, Social Policy, Social Work, Health Studies, or a related field, with demonstrated teaching experience, a strong teaching portfolio, and excellent English communication skills. The role is distinctive for its explicit emphasis on teaching quality within a department known for balancing both teaching and research excellence.

title: Senior Administrative Officer
description: This Senior Administrative Officer role oversees all departmental operations within Philosophy, supervising administrative staff and acting as the primary liaison between administrative teams and departmental leadership. Key responsibilities include coordinating undergraduate and postgraduate programme administration, ensuring operational consistency, and maintaining institutional knowledge during staff transitions. The position requires strong management and coordination capabilities, with a focus on supporting the Department's academic mission and strategic development. The role is distinctive in enabling the Department's international profile and expanding portfolio of academic events and external engagement activities.

title: Assistant Student Affairs Manager I
description: This position supports the integration of non-local students into the university community and Hong Kong society through culturally inclusive programming and cross-cultural initiatives. The role encompasses developing and managing student events, maintaining relationships with international student organizations, and coordinating programs such as cultural ambassador schemes and host family networks. The ideal candidate should be committed to fostering whole-person development and building an inclusive campus environment. The opportunity is distinctive for its focus on internationalization within an institution positioning itself as a leader in human-centric technology and social innovation.

title: Senior Project Officer
description: This Senior Project Officer role supports the Dean of the School of Data Science through administrative management, event coordination, and project oversight, including budgeting and grant proposal development. The position requires a Bachelor's degree and at least four years of tertiary education experience, along with exceptional bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, both written and verbal. Key responsibilities include liaising with stakeholders, establishing research partnerships with Mainland institutions (requiring international travel), and managing complex collaborative projects that advance the university's strategic goals in human-centric technology and social policy. The ideal candidate will demonstrate strong proposal writing capabilities and a proven ability to build institutional partnerships while supporting organizational transformation initiatives.

title: Assistant Programme Manager II / Senior Programme Officer
description: This position manages administrative operations for a newly established doctoral artificial intelligence programme, including course scheduling, budgeting, quality assurance, and stakeholder coordination. The role encompasses teaching coordination, experiential learning organization, financial compliance, and programme reporting for internal and external reviews. The successful candidate must balance operational efficiency with strategic programme development while supporting students, faculty, and external partners. The position is distinctive for its involvement in a transformative initiative centred on human-centric technology and innovation within a rapidly evolving field.

title: Senior Programme Officer
description: This role supports a newly established Artificial Intelligence division as a Senior Programme Officer, managing comprehensive administrative functions including proposal development, admissions, financial oversight, and quality assurance. The position encompasses operational management of teaching and learning, student services including registration and visa processing, scheduling, events, and budgeting. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree with at least four years of tertiary education experience and demonstrate fluency in English and Putonghua. The position is distinctive for supporting an innovative, interdisciplinary AI division within an institution undergoing digital transformation, emphasizing human-centric technology approaches.

title: HPC System Administrator
description: This HPC System Administrator role centers on managing high-performance computing infrastructure and supporting faculty and student researchers through daily operations, Linux administration, user account management, system monitoring, and technical support. Required qualifications include a bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field alongside extensive experience administering large-scale Linux environments across multiple distributions (RHEL, Rocky, Ubuntu, Debian), with preferred knowledge of HPC job schedulers like Slurm and containerization tools such as Docker and Singularity. The position is distinctive for bridging traditional systems administration with specialized HPC expertise to support the institution's strategic expansion into data science and interdisciplinary research initiatives. Strong communication skills are essential for effectively supporting diverse research users.

title: Senior Technical Officer / Technical Officer
department: Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries
description: This position provides technical support to a creative arts department, managing specialized equipment across film production, animation, motion capture, sound, and immersive media technologies. The successful candidate must possess strong technical literacy and computational skills, with demonstrated ability to troubleshoot problems independently, master new equipment quickly, and perform preventative maintenance while assisting faculty and instructors. The role requires effective cross-functional communication with IT services and facilities management to maintain operational continuity of technical resources. What distinguishes this opportunity is the chance to work at the convergence of creative education and cutting-edge technology within a dynamic and expanding creative industries field.

title: Administrative Officer
description: This Administrative Officer role supports a Master's programme in International and Development Economics, with primary duties encompassing application processing, student enquiries, promotional coordination, and records management. The position requires a minimum of two years' work experience, ideally in higher education, along with fluent English and Mandarin Chinese communication skills and demonstrated teamwork abilities. Candidates with backgrounds in development economics, business, or prior course administration experience are preferred. The role involves occasional travel and flexible hours to meet programme demands.

title: Counsellor
description: The Counsellor position in the Office of Student Affairs involves providing individual and group psychological counselling to students, including those with special needs, and conducting psychological assessments to develop tailored support plans. Key responsibilities include designing and delivering wellness programmes, coordinating university-wide mental health initiatives, and supporting student integration across diverse backgrounds. The role requires supervising support staff and advising parents and university personnel on student wellbeing. This is a distinctive position emphasizing whole-person development within a transforming institution combining traditional strengths in arts and social sciences with emerging interdisciplinary research initiatives.

title: (Senior) Administrative Officer, Undergraduate Programmes Office
description: This administrative role supports an undergraduate programmes office within a business faculty, managing admissions, student advising, quality assurance, and accreditation processes. The successful candidate will need a degree and at least five years of administrative experience—ideally in higher education—with proficiency in admissions, event coordination, and advanced computer skills. Bilingual capability in English and Cantonese or Putonghua is required, along with strong analytical and collaborative abilities. The position offers competitive compensation with standard benefits including leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Administrative Officer
description: The Administrative Officer will deliver broad administrative support to a Philosophy Department, with emphasis on a newly established 2-year undergraduate programme and research operations. Core duties encompass assisting the Head of Department with programme planning and coordination, organizing departmental events and seminars, managing finances and budgets, and providing secretarial services to departmental leadership and committees. The role involves stakeholder liaison, coordinating professional development initiatives such as internships and workshops, and producing programme reports. The position is distinguished by its location within a well-established philosophy department at a research-intensive institution that prioritizes interdisciplinary engagement and human-centered innovation.

title: Assistant Executive Officer (Office Admin & General Service Team)
description: This administrative position supports a graduate studies office through daily operations management, student services, and coordination of meetings and events. The role requires at least three years of administrative experience preferably in higher education, strong analytical and problem-solving skills, bilingual competency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua), and advanced proficiency in MS Office. Key responsibilities include providing counter services to postgraduate students and stakeholders, managing budgeting and human resources matters, and ensuring efficient office operations. The position is distinctive for integrating multiple administrative functions within a research-focused graduate school environment, with particular emphasis on enhancing postgraduate education quality.

title: Senior Information Security Analyst
description: This Senior Information Security Analyst role manages the institution's security operations by conducting security testing and threat modeling throughout the software development lifecycle while operating advanced security tools including SAST, DAST, and SIEM platforms. Key responsibilities encompass investigating cyber incidents, ensuring data protection compliance, and delivering cybersecurity awareness training across the organization. The position is distinctive in bridging both proactive and reactive security functions—providing security architecture guidance and supporting a Security Champions Program alongside threat intelligence analysis and incident response. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree and demonstrate current expertise in information security practices and emerging threats.

title: Information Manager (Virtualisation Services)
description: This role manages critical virtualization and storage infrastructure, including server systems, hyperconverged infrastructure, SAN storage, and IoT platforms, with duties spanning capacity planning, lifecycle management, backup policies, and network support services. The position demands a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or IT with at least 8 years of IT experience—preferably including 3 years in management—and demonstrated expertise in enterprise virtualization technologies. The role is distinctive for its involvement in emerging technologies including container orchestration and large-scale campus IoT deployment, bridging operational infrastructure with innovation initiatives. The position requires shift work and irregular hours due to the mission-critical nature of infrastructure support.

title: Assistant Accounting Manager I
description: This Assistant Accounting Manager I position in the Finance Office requires supervising an accounting team responsible for monthly payroll cycles, compiling staff cost budgets across departments, and improving workflow efficiency. The ideal candidate holds an accounting degree and professional certification (HKICPA/ACCA), with at least 10 years of accounting experience including 3+ years in management, strong Excel and accounting software skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities under pressure. Prior experience in higher education or the non-profit sector is preferred. The role offers competitive salary and benefits including annual leave, medical/dental coverage, and retirement contributions, with review continuing until the position is filled.

title: Senior Administrative Manager (Research Postgraduate Team)
description: This role oversees administrative operations for research postgraduate programmes, managing admissions, course registration, student records, and financial support while supervising day-to-day programme operations. The position requires at least 10 years of post-qualification experience, ideally in tertiary education, alongside strong analytical, interpersonal, and communication capabilities, with fluency in both English and Chinese including Putonghua. Key responsibilities include providing administrative support for academic development and quality assurance, and contributing to academic policy formulation and programme reviews. The role is distinctive for its focus on research postgraduate programmes within an institution emphasising interdisciplinary research and global academic collaboration.

title: Visiting English Tutor
department: Centre for English and Additional Languages
description: This visiting position involves delivering English language support through tutorials, workshops, and self-access materials to undergraduate and postgraduate students at a dedicated language centre. Key responsibilities include organizing academic and social events that develop English skills while fostering cultural exchange between international tutors and local students. Candidates must hold a recent Bachelor's or Master's degree from designated partner institutions and demonstrate advanced English proficiency (IELTS 8.0 or above). The role is distinctive for its emphasis on building interpersonal relationships and cultural interaction alongside formal language instruction, actively seeking applicants committed to meaningful engagement with the student community.

title: Associate Director (Application Development)
description: This role leads strategic application development for enterprise-level systems supporting academic, administrative, and research operations, reporting directly to the CIO & University Librarian. The Associate Director manages a team of over 16 developers, overseeing the complete software development lifecycle and directing a major migration to a new Enterprise Resource Planning system. The position requires a leader with strong technical expertise, proven management capabilities, and the ability to champion secure, scalable, user-focused solutions across departments. Success demands both technical depth and cross-functional collaboration to address complex institutional technology needs.

title: Assistant Administrative Officer (TPg Programme Team)
description: This position supports the School of Graduate Studies by providing comprehensive administrative assistance to taught postgraduate programmes, including student admission, data management, teaching coordination, examinations, and quality assurance. The role encompasses counter services, office administration, and student activity support. Candidates must demonstrate strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua) and competency with Microsoft Office and web applications. The appointment is contract-based for up to 11 months, with salary commensurate with qualifications and experience, and includes standard benefits such as annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and mandatory provident fund contributions.

title: Senior Research Officer/Research Officer
department: Department of Sociology and Social Policy
description: This position involves conducting data analysis and research support for a longitudinal study examining discrimination and health-risk behavior within the Department of Sociology and Social Policy. The successful candidate must hold a master's degree in social or behavioral sciences and demonstrate strong proficiency in statistical programming (Stata, Mplus, or R) alongside solid quantitative research methodology skills. Preference will be given to candidates with expertise in health or aging research and experience handling large-scale or panel datasets. The role is offered on a 12-month fixed-term contract with remuneration based on qualifications and experience, including standard benefits such as leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Assistant Research Officer
description: This position is an Assistant Research Officer within the Department of Accountancy, requiring knowledge of textual analysis using Python or similar programming languages. The role is contract-based initially for up to six months with potential renewal, and the salary will be determined by qualifications and experience. The appointment includes standard benefits such as annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and mandatory provident fund contributions. The position supports the university's commitment to interdisciplinary research and innovation, particularly within its broader initiative to establish itself as a hub for human-centric technology and social policy development.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Institute of Insurance and Risk Management
description: This Research Assistant Professor position at a newly established insurance and risk management institute requires conducting scholarly research, securing external funding, and teaching at both undergraduate and postgraduate levels while contributing to institutional service. Applicants must hold a PhD in Risk and Insurance or related field earned within the past five years and demonstrate strong research credentials. The role is distinctive for its emphasis on practice-oriented research that connects academic work directly to industry applications, including organizing forums, conferences, and applied surveys designed to support the region's insurance sector development.

title: Assistant Professor of Teaching
department: Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries
description: This is a teaching-focused assistant professor position in digital animation, VR/AR, interactive art, or cinematography, requiring the ability to teach at both undergraduate and postgraduate levels and supervise capstone projects. The role emphasizes teaching excellence within an interdisciplinary creative department that integrates film, animation, digital media, and curatorial studies to support the region's growing creative industries. The position is distinctive for its emphasis on human-centric technology and innovation, positioning the successful candidate as a bridge between emerging digital technologies and creative practice. Key requirements include expertise in contemporary digital technologies and creative methodologies to contribute to departmental development and institutional transformation.

title: Assistant Library Officer (Cataloguing and Preservation)
description: This role focuses on cataloguing and preserving library materials using international standards and the Alma Library System, while maintaining bibliographic records and compiling relevant statistics, with additional counter service duties including evening and weekend shifts. Candidates should possess cataloguing experience in an academic library, proficiency in Resource Description & Access and MARC21 standards, and fluency in both English and Chinese. Experience with integrated library systems, particularly Alma/Primo, is preferred but not essential. The position offers competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits including annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement provisions, with openness to candidates at various career stages.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow - Philosophy of Contemporary & Future Science
description: This postdoctoral position supports a five-year research fellowship in philosophy of contemporary and future science, offering one to two-year appointments to candidates with expertise in philosophy of science, formal epistemology, AI, or philosophy of specific sciences. The successful candidate will engage in interdisciplinary collaboration with both philosophers and scientists, contributing to high-impact research within a leading philosophy department recognized across Asia for research excellence and top-tier publication output. The role is distinctive for its positioning within a broader institutional shift toward human-centric technology and social policy research, offering fellows the chance to work within an active postdoctoral community while addressing urgent philosophical questions at the intersection of science and emerging technologies.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer
description: The Department of Translation seeks a Senior Lecturer or Lecturer to teach courses in computer-aided translation, business translation, interpreting, translation technology, and cultural studies, bridging professional practice with academic rigor in bilingual Chinese-English contexts. Candidates must hold a master's degree in Translation Studies or a related field and demonstrate fluent command of both Chinese and English, with additional proficiency in Cantonese and Putonghua preferred; Senior Lecturer applicants require at least eight years of university teaching experience. The position is distinctive for its integrated approach treating translation as both a practical profession and scholarly discipline, positioned within an institution developing into a center for human-centric technology and interdisciplinary research. Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits including medical, dental, and relocation support are provided.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Office of the Faculty of Business
description: This position is a Research Assistant Professor within the Faculty of Business, requiring the holder to conduct independent and collaborative research while teaching undergraduate and postgraduate courses. The ideal candidate will hold a PhD in management science, operations research, or a related field earned within the past five years, with demonstrated research excellence and quality publications. The role involves working across the Office of the Faculty of Business and the Hong Kong Institute of Business Studies, with potential administrative responsibilities. This is a distinctive opportunity within an institution emphasizing human-centric technology, social policies, and interdisciplinary collaboration through its advanced research institute.

title: Part-time Lecturer
department: Centre for English and Additional Languages
description: This part-time position seeks an Academic English Language Lecturer to teach undergraduate courses focused on developing English proficiency and critical thinking skills within a center dedicated to language support. Candidates must have university-level teaching experience, preferably in Hong Kong, and hold at least a master's degree in English Language and Literature or a related field, with a PhD preferred. The role emphasizes expertise in foundational academic English instruction and advanced skills such as academic writing, critical argumentation, seminar discussion, and reflective practices. Compensation is competitive and based on qualifications and experience, with appointments made on a contract basis.

title: Sr. Administrative Officer / Administrative Officer
department: LEO Dr David P. Chan Institute of Data Science
description: This administrative role provides comprehensive support to a data science institute director, encompassing event management, stakeholder relationship building, and funding development alongside operational functions such as budgeting, procurement, and financial oversight. The position requires a Bachelor's degree with a minimum of 5 years' experience in education (or less for the Officer-level position), bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese including Putonghua, and technical skills in Microsoft PowerPoint, Adobe Creative Suite, and Chinese word processing. Success in the role demands strong teamwork, initiative, analytical ability, and the capacity to manage multiple concurrent responsibilities under tight deadlines while maintaining regulatory compliance.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Department of Sociology and Social Policy
description: This Research Assistant Professor position requires a PhD in Sociology, Social Policy, or computational social science obtained within the last five years, along with demonstrated expertise in both teaching and research. The role prioritizes research productivity and publication output, with applicants expected to provide a comprehensive research portfolio, publication record, and teaching evaluations. The department is internationally recognized for research excellence and success in securing competitive grants, and the position supports the university's strategic emphasis on interdisciplinary and human-centric research. Candidates must possess strong communication skills and be committed to enhancing the department's research profile while contributing to undergraduate and postgraduate teaching.

title: Assistant Administrative Officer
description: This Assistant Administrative Officer position in the Department of Economics provides comprehensive administrative support for undergraduate programs, including admissions, academic advising, assessments, and student services. Key responsibilities include coordinating student and teaching activities, maintaining departmental records, and managing admission interviews, visa applications, and registration processes. The role requires proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and webpage technology. The position is offered on a 12-month contract basis with potential for reappointment based on performance, with compensation and benefits determined by qualifications and experience.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer
department: Department of Sociology and Social Policy
description: This position is a teaching and research role in sociology and social policy at a Hong Kong university, requiring the successful candidate to deliver undergraduate courses in health and social care, fundraising, and program management. Applicants must possess a PhD in Sociology, Social Policy, Social Work, Health Studies, or a closely related discipline, along with proven teaching experience and strong English proficiency. The department is research-active with strong international publication credentials and grant-winning capacity, expecting new faculty to contribute to both undergraduate and postgraduate education. The role is distinctive for its alignment with the institution's mission to develop human-centric technology and social policy in response to digital transformation.

title: Associate Director/Senior Manager/Manager
description: This role involves developing and executing the university's internationalisation strategy, including building and maintaining international partnerships and overseeing student mobility programmes. The successful candidate will also be responsible for reviewing and enhancing international student recruitment initiatives. Strong interpersonal, communication, and leadership skills are essential, alongside experience in higher education administration. The position offers flexibility in rank and salary based on qualifications, with competitive benefits including leave, medical coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Director of Research and Knowledge Transfer
department: Office of Research and Knowledge Transfer
description: This Director position leads the Office of Research and Knowledge Transfer, overseeing research strategy development, funding applications, partnership negotiations, and the commercialization of research outputs to drive institutional impact and societal benefit. The role requires a minimum of 15 years in higher education with proven expertise in research strategy, grant management, and knowledge transfer, combined with strong corporate reporting capabilities and multilingual proficiency in English, Chinese, and Putonghua. The position is distinctive for its strategic focus on positioning the institution as a global leader in human-centric technology and social policy innovation, with emphasis on international collaboration and regional research development. This is a senior leadership role advising executive management on research policy while championing technology commercialization and policy innovation initiatives.

title: Administrative Officer /Assistant Administrative Officer (TPg Admission)
description: This role supports the School of Graduate Studies by managing administrative operations for taught postgraduate programmes, with primary responsibilities including student admissions, registration, document verification, and coordination of outreach activities to mainland China. The position also handles financial tasks such as purchases, reimbursements, and budgeting, while the Administrative Officer level additionally supervises junior staff. Candidates require at least two years of experience in tertiary education administration, preferably in postgraduate admissions and programme promotion, alongside fluent English and Chinese (including Putonghua). The role demands strong initiative, the ability to work under pressure, and collaborative teamwork with a high sense of responsibility.

title: Research Assistant Professor
description: The position is a Research Assistant Professor at the Hong Kong Catastrophic Risk Centre, a philosophy-focused research unit seeking someone with expertise in philosophy of AI and/or ethics of risk. The successful candidate must hold a PhD obtained within the past five years and demonstrate a publication record in leading philosophy journals. The role offers the opportunity to conduct high-impact research within a department recognized as one of Asia's strongest in analytic philosophy, while contributing to the centre's mission of addressing catastrophic risks through philosophical inquiry.

title: Assistant Administrative Manager II/Sr. Administrative Officer
description: This role supports a newly established Division of Artificial Intelligence, requiring an administrative leader to manage external affairs, develop branding strategies, and oversee publicity across multiple channels including the division's website. The successful candidate must hold a Bachelor's degree with at least 5 years of tertiary education experience, combined with bilingual English and Chinese proficiency and strong organizational and communication skills. Key responsibilities include event organization, budget preparation, reporting, and disseminating the division's research achievements to diverse audiences. This distinctive opportunity offers the chance to build administrative infrastructure for an emerging research division within a university positioning itself as a hub for human-centric technology and social policy innovation.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
description: The position is a tenure-track role in the Department of English specializing in Modern Literature, requiring the successful candidate to teach undergraduate and postgraduate courses while supervising student projects, conduct high-impact research and secure competitive grants, and contribute to departmental administration and curriculum development. The appointee should demonstrate strong teaching effectiveness and substantial potential for quality research output in the near term. Candidates with experience in digital humanities, modern literature, or world literatures are particularly valued. The institution emphasizes excellence in both teaching and research, with competitive compensation and benefits packages commensurate with qualifications and experience.

title: Manager (Technology Transfer and Business Development)
department: Office of Research and Knowledge Transfer
description: This position manages technology transfer and commercialization strategies, overseeing intellectual property portfolios, identifying patentable innovations, and marketing inventions to industry partners across Hong Kong, mainland China, and international markets. The manager will negotiate licensing agreements, organize technology marketing events, maintain transfer databases and websites, and produce related marketing materials and reports. The role requires a bachelor's degree in science or engineering with demonstrated experience in technology transfer, business development, and event management, along with strong English and Chinese communication skills. The appointment level and compensation are flexible based on candidate qualifications, with benefits including competitive salary, leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Professor of Teaching / Associate Professor of Teaching / Assistant Professor of Teaching
description: This teaching-focused position in the Marketing and International Business Department is available at Assistant, Associate, or Professor rank for candidates with a doctoral degree in Marketing or a related field. Primary responsibilities encompass teaching core and elective courses, developing innovative curricula and pedagogical approaches, coordinating programmes, mentoring students, and engaging in knowledge transfer activities. The role emphasises educational impact and holistic student development, with encouragement to pursue teaching grants and drive curriculum innovation. Appointment rank and salary are commensurate with qualifications and experience, and the position offers comprehensive benefits including leave, medical and dental coverage, and relocation support.

title: Professor
department: Department of Marketing and International Business
description: This Professor position requires a PhD in Marketing or related field with an established track record of publishing in top-tier journals and securing competitive research grants. The role combines active research leadership with teaching responsibilities across undergraduate and postgraduate levels, alongside contributions to departmental strategy and curriculum development. Prior administrative experience and success in pursuing RGC and international funding are valued attributes. The appointment is structured as an initial three-year contract with potential for longer-term substantiation.

title: Senior Research Engineer
description: This Senior Research Engineer role develops humanitarian technologies through an academic UN Hub, applying AI, data analytics, and IoT to address challenges in developing regions and support sustainable development goals. The position requires strong expertise in AI and data analytics alongside a demonstrated commitment to humanitarian applications, and involves collaborating with interdisciplinary teams to translate research concepts into practical solutions for the Global South. A distinctive aspect is the integration of teaching responsibilities, including lectures for a Master's programme in global humanitarian innovation, combining research with academic mentorship. The role emphasizes human-centric, impact-oriented technology development across diverse applications from medical devices to environmental solutions.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
description: This position seeks a PhD-qualified finance scholar to teach and conduct research within a liberal arts framework, combining undergraduate and postgraduate instruction with scholarly publication in top-tier journals. Candidates must demonstrate strong research potential, secure external funding from competitive sources, and contribute to departmental service. Preferred candidates will have an established publication record, professional certifications like CFA, prior teaching or administrative experience, and connections to Hong Kong's financial sector, alongside excellent communication abilities.

title: Professor / Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
description: The Department of History seeks a Professor, Associate Professor, or Assistant Professor specializing in Asia in global contexts, with a PhD in History or related field and a strong record of peer-reviewed publications and teaching excellence. The successful candidate must contribute to the department's strategic priorities in global Asia, public history, and/or digital history, with digital humanities experience considered advantageous. Key responsibilities include conducting high-impact research, securing competitive grant funding, and developing innovative courses. The position offers competitive remuneration commensurate with qualifications, along with comprehensive benefits including leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Research Assistant Professor
description: This Research Assistant Professor position seeks an early-career historian specializing in global history, particularly from underrepresented regions such as South Asia, Southeast Asia, Europe, and the Americas, or possessing expertise in digital historical methods. Applicants must hold a PhD obtained within the past five years, demonstrate a strong track record of publications and grant acquisition, and teach two to three courses annually while contributing to institutional service. The role prioritizes scholarly productivity through international publications and competitive research funding, with preference given to candidates combining digital research capabilities with innovative teaching experience.

title: Assistant Professor
description: The Department of History seeks an Assistant Professor specializing in United States history with a transnational or global perspective, requiring a PhD in History or a related field. The successful candidate must demonstrate excellence in research and teaching, with ability to teach U.S. history courses and contribute to departmental priorities including global Asia, public history, and digital history; experience in digital humanities is advantageous. The position is tenure-track with an initial three-year contract potentially leading to longer-term appointment and substantiation, offering competitive remuneration and comprehensive benefits. The appointee will be expected to actively seek external funding through RGC grants and international competitive schemes.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries
description: The position is a fixed-term Research Assistant Professor role in the Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries focused on teaching and advancing the MA in Creative and Media Industries programme. The appointee will teach graduate-level courses, supervise capstone projects, and contribute to departmental development and service. The ideal candidate holds a PhD in film and media studies with a strong publication record, research awards, and success in securing competitive grants.

title: Senior Administrative Officer / Administrative Officer
description: This position supports the Division of Artificial Intelligence by providing comprehensive administrative services, including financial management, procurement, reimbursements, and budgeting. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree with relevant tertiary education sector experience (minimum four years for the Senior level) and demonstrates fluent written and verbal English and Chinese proficiency, alongside strong analytical and interpersonal skills. The role is distinctive in supporting a newly established division within a university undergoing transformation into a hub for human-centric technology research and innovation. Competitive remuneration is offered alongside standard fringe benefits including leave, healthcare coverage, and pension contributions.

title: Research Assistant Professor
description: This Research Assistant Professor position seeks a philosopher with expertise in Set Theory or strong teaching credentials in logic-related courses such as Critical Thinking, Philosophy of Artificial Intelligence, or Deductive Logic. The role requires a PhD in Philosophy or a related field and involves teaching specialized coursework while potentially freeing departmental colleagues to focus on Set Theory instruction. The position offers involvement with newly launched philosophy programs, including a Bachelor of Arts in Philosophy and AI and a Master of Arts in Artificial Intelligence and the Future, within a department recognized for excellence in analytic philosophy across aesthetics, ethics, Chinese philosophy, and philosophy of mind.

title: Research Officer
department: The Advanced Institute for Global Chinese Studies
description: This Research Officer position supports the director of an institute focused on global Chinese studies by coordinating research projects across humanities disciplines, with emphasis on Chinese literature and culture. Key responsibilities include organizing seminars and international conferences, producing video content on Chinese topics, editing publications, and managing external communications with contributors and media partners. Candidates should demonstrate experience in project design and operational management, and the role offers competitive salary and comprehensive benefits including leave, medical coverage, and retirement contributions. The position is based at a Hong Kong institution committed to interdisciplinary research and global scholarly collaboration.

title: Chair Professor
description: This senior academic leadership position in Accountancy requires a PhD holder with extensive teaching experience and a strong international research profile demonstrated through high-impact publications and secured grants. The role combines research and teaching responsibilities with departmental and faculty management duties, emphasizing alignment with the institution's strategic priorities in AI, data science, and STEM-related accounting research. Candidates with prior administrative experience at departmental or faculty level are preferred. The appointee must maintain excellence across teaching, research, and service while supporting faculty development and contributing to institutional initiatives.

title: Senior Programme Officer / Programme Officer
description: This position supports the Doctor of Artificial Intelligence Studies program, requiring management of course scheduling, student logistics, budgeting, and stakeholder relationships across academic and administrative functions. The role demands a bachelor's degree and at least five years of experience in program management, administrative support, or event coordination in tertiary or executive education settings. Key responsibilities include organizing outreach events, coordinating teaching activities, handling financial tasks, and serving as the primary student contact. The ideal candidate is self-motivated, proactive, with strong project management capabilities and experience managing high-level programs or events.

title: Senior Programme Officer / Programme Officer
description: This position involves managing administrative and promotional activities for academic programs within a data science division, including developing marketing materials, updating digital platforms, organizing events, and creating content for social media campaigns to increase engagement and visibility. Candidates should hold a bachelor's degree in marketing, public relations, event management, business administration, or a related field, with at least four years of relevant experience for the senior level role. Essential skills include proficiency with Microsoft Office and Adobe Creative Suite, alongside strong organizational abilities and multitasking capabilities. Preference is given to applicants with knowledge of the higher education sector in Hong Kong and mainland China.

title: Associate Professor of Teaching / Assistant Professor of Teaching / Senior Lecturer
description: The position is for a teaching-focused Associate/Assistant Professor or Senior Lecturer within the School of Graduate Studies, specializing in internationalisation and quality management in higher education. The role encompasses teaching and learning activities, academic program coordination and management, organization of research events and knowledge transfer activities, and related administrative duties. Candidates must hold a PhD in a related discipline and demonstrate at least five years of relevant teaching experience with a track record in academic program development. This appointment supports the university's strategic expansion of postgraduate education and its emphasis on interdisciplinary research, particularly in policy studies, health studies, and higher education.

title: Associate Professor/Assistant Professor
description: The position is an Associate/Assistant Professor role in Artificial Intelligence focused on conducting high-quality research in areas such as natural language processing, large language models, and computer vision, while securing external research funding. Responsibilities include teaching undergraduate and postgraduate courses in data science and AI, managing admissions and programme operations, and performing administrative duties. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in a relevant field with a strong publication and citation record, good command of both English and Chinese, and ideally possess experience in grant applications and academic programme administration; Associate Professor applicants should have at least six years of tenure-track academic experience.

title: Senior Administrative Officer / Administrative Officer
department: Institute of Insurance and Risk Management
description: This role provides administrative support to the Institute of Insurance and Risk Management and the Department of Operations and Risk Management, handling various departmental activities and operations. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree with at least three years of administrative experience, ideally in higher education, alongside strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (Mandarin) and demonstrated computer literacy, including website maintenance skills. The position requires the ability to work both collaboratively within teams and independently, with salary and rank determined competitively based on qualifications and experience. Additional benefits include annual leave, medical and dental coverage, mandatory provident fund contributions, and gratuity.

title: Part-time Clerk
description: This part-time clerk position offers flexible scheduling options (25-30 hours weekly or 18 hours weekly) with potential weekend and evening shifts at a central Hong Kong location, supporting a self-financing further education institute. Candidates must have completed secondary school as a minimum, with preference for those holding an associate degree or higher diploma, and must demonstrate proficiency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua) and competency with Microsoft Office and Chinese word processing software. The role is distinctive for its adaptable work arrangements and its placement within an educational institution delivering diverse tertiary and continuing education programmes. Immediate availability is preferred, and salary will be commensurate with qualifications and experience.

title: Senior Programme Officer/Programme Officer,Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office
description: This role manages taught postgraduate programmes in the Faculty of Business, overseeing admissions, registration, scheduling, financial operations, quality assurance, and committee support. Candidates require a Bachelor's degree plus two to four years of tertiary education experience (depending on seniority level), combined with fluent English and Chinese language skills. Key responsibilities include student services coordination from admission to completion, examination and event management, and institutional reporting. The position offers leadership opportunities and the chance to contribute to the institution's strategic development as an interdisciplinary research and innovation centre.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: JC STEM Early Career Research Fellowship for Translation and Application (24/25)
description: This postdoctoral fellowship supports early-career STEM researchers for three years of full-time research at the institution, with potential for an additional year of industry-based translational research attachment. The position offers comprehensive financial support including an annual stipend of up to $530,000 plus a 15% completion gratuity, alongside professional development funding of up to $30,600 annually. The fellowship aims to help researchers advance their careers, expand professional networks, and generate social impact through translational research and innovation. Candidates from around the world are eligible, and the position is particularly suited for researchers interested in bridging academic research with practical, real-world applications in industry settings.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: LU Presidential Postdoctoral Research Fellowship Scheme
description: This postdoctoral fellowship scheme welcomes early-career researchers globally who have received their PhD within the past three years and demonstrated strong research potential and publication records. Fellows will work collaboratively within a supportive research environment across the institution's diverse faculties and interdisciplinary institutes, with access to resources, expertise, and mentorship to advance their research goals. The position offers a three-year appointment structure, beginning with a one-year contract with potential extension for two additional years based on satisfactory performance. Appointees are required to pursue external funding through specified local and international grant schemes, positioning them for independent research careers.

title: Chair Professor / Professor / Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
description: This is a recruitment for tenure-track academic positions across multiple ranks (from Assistant Professor to Chair Professor) in any discipline, with strategic priority given to interdisciplinary scholars connecting STEM with humanities, business, and social sciences, particularly those working on sustainable energy, carbon neutrality, environmental sustainability, and smart infrastructure. Candidates must hold a PhD, with experience and research standing appropriate to their target rank. The university offers competitive compensation and funding packages, including eligibility for government-sponsored STEM professorships and an internal early-career scholars scheme providing enhanced salary and start-up grants.

title: Professor and Director
department: Pan Sutong SH-HK Economic Policy Research Institute
description: This position combines a full professorship in Economics with directorship of an economic policy research institute serving mainland China and Hong Kong. The role demands both strategic leadership of research operations and extensive stakeholder engagement with academic and industry partners, alongside building international collaborative networks. Candidates must demonstrate exceptional scholarship in economic development policy relevant to both regions, alongside proven excellence in research, teaching, and knowledge exchange. The distinctive nature of this role lies in its dual emphasis on rigorous academic work and direct policy influence, positioning the appointee as a bridge between two major economies.

title: Administrative Officer, Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office
description: This administrative position supports postgraduate taught programmes within a business faculty, focusing on stakeholder engagement, digital marketing, and campaign coordination. The successful candidate requires a Bachelor's degree with at least two years of relevant experience, ideally in higher education, alongside bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (Putonghua). Key competencies include strong communication and teamwork abilities, analytical skills, and the capacity to work independently under tight deadlines across diverse responsibilities. The role offers competitive remuneration with comprehensive benefits including annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement provisions.

title: 實驗室服務員
description: The School of Biomedical Sciences seeks a Laboratory Assistant on a two-year contract or one-year temporary basis, with the possibility of renewal based on satisfactory performance. Applicants must have Form 3 education or equivalent, with basic written proficiency in Chinese and English and good communication skills; prior relevant experience is preferred. The role involves cleaning offices, laboratories, and equipment, as well as handling general administrative and field duties. Starting salary is determined by educational background and work experience, with benefits including paid leave, medical coverage, and end-of-contract gratuity plus mandatory provident fund contributions (10% of total salary) for two-year contract holders.

title: Clerk I (holding the functional title of Senior Executive Assistant)
description: This Senior Executive Assistant position in the School of Biomedical Sciences is a two-year fixed-term role supporting education programme administration, academic events, alumni outreach, and various ad hoc tasks. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree with at least two years of work experience, demonstrate bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, and possess strong organizational, communication, and IT skills alongside self-motivation and teamwork abilities. The role offers competitive salary packages including retirement benefits contributions (up to 10% of basic salary), annual leave, and medical benefits, with the possibility of contract renewal subject to performance and funding. Applications are accepted online with ongoing review until April 30, 2026, or until the position is filled.

title: Post-doctoral Fellow (several posts) at the Centre for Quantitative History (CQH)
description: These are several post-doctoral positions at a research centre focused on quantitative approaches to economic, financial, and social history, with particular emphasis on China. Appointees will conduct independent research leading to academic publications, supervise research assistants, and manage centre activities including seminars and conferences. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Economics, Finance, History, or related fields completed within the past three years, with demonstrated expertise in quantitative history and fluency in both written and spoken English and Chinese. Positions are offered on one-year or two-year fixed-term contracts with competitive salaries, benefits, and potential renewal, with applications reviewed on a rolling basis until August 2026.

title: Research Assistant I/II (several posts) in the Centre for Quantitative History (CQH)
description: This Research Assistant position at the Centre for Quantitative History involves supporting principal investigators on economic, financial, and social history research projects, with primary focus on data collection, compilation, and verification to ensure research accuracy. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in economics, finance, history, or related disciplines, with strong English and Chinese language skills, econometrics knowledge, and computer literacy; quantitative experience with tools like Stata, R, GIS, or Python is advantageous. The role is particularly suited for individuals planning to pursue PhDs in economics or economic history who seek practical experience in rigorous research environments and are comfortable with meticulous, detail-oriented data work. The position offers a competitive salary, benefits including annual leave and medical coverage, and contract-end gratuity, with one-year temporary appointments renewable based on performance.

title: E-learning Assistant (at the rank of Research Assistant I/II)
department: Li Ka Shing Faculty of Medicine (20000)
description: This two-year fixed-term position supports the Faculty of Medicine's e-learning initiatives, combining educational technology expertise with content development and pedagogical support. The role involves collaborating with faculty, students, and multimedia professionals to integrate emerging technologies such as GenAI, AR/VR, and web-based tools into teaching and learning activities. Candidates should hold a postgraduate degree in biology, biomedical sciences, medicine, or related fields, with strong English proficiency and preferably prior experience in project management and e-learning applications. Web development or multimedia production skills are advantageous but not essential, and applicants with lower qualifications may be considered for the Research Assistant II tier.

department: Restorative Dental Sciences of the Faculty of Dentistry (08026)
description: This position combines full-stack web development with research responsibilities in a dental sciences faculty, requiring candidates to design and maintain responsive web applications while researching user behavior and emerging technologies to support learning management system development. title: Part-time Research Assistant I/II (holding a functional title of UX/UI designer) in the Division of Restorative Dental Sciences of the Faculty of Dentistry
department: Restorative Dental Sciences of the Faculty of Dentistry (08026)
description: This part-time research assistant position in dental sciences seeks a UX/UI designer to develop responsive websites and web applications with emphasis on user experience and accessibility. The role involves conducting user research, designing interactive interfaces, collaborating with stakeholders to translate requirements into functional designs, and maintaining applications with attention to security and cross-browser compatibility. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree in computer science or related field with at least two years of frontend development experience, proficiency in HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript, and expertise in modern frameworks such as Vue.js or React, supported by a strong design portfolio. Experience with Learning Management Systems like Moodle or Blackboard, or with integrating AI-driven features, is particularly desirable.

title: Senior Executive Assistant/ Executive Assistant (at the rank of Clerk I/II)
department: School of Computing and Data Science (14700)
description: This position supports a rapidly expanding computing and data science school as an Executive Assistant, requiring a Bachelor's degree, at least 3 years of work experience, and bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua). The role centers on coordinating admissions outreach, developing marketing campaigns across online and offline channels, creating promotional materials, and building engagement with the student community. The ideal candidate is a self-motivated, organized team player with admissions and student liaison experience who can manage multiple priorities independently in a fast-paced environment and work flexible hours as needed. The position is offered on a two-year fixed-term contract with competitive compensation and end-of-contract gratuity.

title: Assistant Registrar (to be seconded to GBAICTI as its Vice President (Research Strategy Development & Application))
department: Li Ka Shing Faculty of Medicine (20000)
description: This Assistant Registrar position is seconded to a government-backed clinical research institute as Vice President of Research Strategy Development & Application, where the successful candidate will direct research infrastructure development, manage funding schemes, and evaluate emerging research methodologies while advising researchers and industry partners. The role requires a bachelor's degree or higher in medicine, pharmacy, biotechnology or related fields, coupled with at least 10 years of professional and supervisory experience in clinical research, with demonstrated expertise in clinical development strategies, regulatory requirements, and research infrastructure management. The position is distinctive for its strategic influence in shaping clinical research policy and infrastructure across Hong Kong and the Greater Bay Area at a regional level. The role reports directly to the CEO, offering significant leadership responsibility in advancing the institute's research capabilities and partnerships.

title: Clerk I / II (holding the functional title of Senior Executive Assistant / Executive Assistant)
description: This temporary or fixed-term administrative position supports a School of Chinese Medicine through programme administration, event coordination, enquiry handling, and record-keeping, requiring flexibility including work outside standard office hours. Candidates must hold 5 Hong Kong secondary examination passes (including English, Chinese, and Mathematics) or equivalent, with at least 4 years of full-time work experience, strong bilingual English-Chinese proficiency, computer literacy, and solid interpersonal skills. A Bachelor's degree qualifies applicants for the Executive Assistant title, while higher qualifications and experience may lead to consideration for the Senior Executive Assistant role. The position offers competitive salary and standard benefits with potential renewal based on performance.

Essential qualifications include a PhD in Civil/Structural Engineering, a strong publication and research funding record in composite/smart structures, university teaching experience, and proficiency with FEM software (ABAQUS, ANSYS, SAP2000) and programming languages (Python/MATLAB). Professional engineering licensure (HKIE/CEng) or eligibility is advantageous, and candidates at senior levels should demonstrate established international research reputation.

The fixed-term position, renewable based on performance, begins in August 2026 and offers an opportunity to join a diverse, internationally-recognised department ranked 18th globally.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This position supports research at the intersection of generative AI and architectural heritage, focusing on Lingnan water-adaptive architecture through multi-scalar morphological analysis. The Research Assistant will conduct heritage analysis, develop specialized image datasets with performance-based captioning, fine-tune AI models, and coordinate broader research activities including publications and workshops. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in Architecture, Urban Design, or Data Science with proficiency in GIS and Adobe Suite; experience with generative AI tools and machine learning is highly desirable. The role is initially a three-month renewable contract, with exceptional candidates considered for doctoral study opportunities.

title: Laboratory Assistant
description: This Laboratory Assistant position in the nursing school involves preparing clinical skills equipment, maintaining the simulation centre, supporting school events, and performing administrative tasks like record-keeping and data entry. Candidates must have completed secondary education with specific qualifications in Chinese and English plus three additional subjects, basic computer proficiency, strong interpersonal skills, and the ability to work independently; prior experience with nursing equipment is advantageous. The role is distinctive for combining technical laboratory support with administrative responsibilities in a clinical education setting. Initial appointment is for one year, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Educational Administration and Policy
description: The position is a Research Assistant supporting a project investigating school networks and system leadership across selected schools in the Greater Bay Area. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree or higher in Education or Social Sciences with at least one year of research experience in educational contexts, along with fluent English and Chinese language skills and proficiency in survey design, interviews, and data analysis. Key responsibilities include conducting literature reviews, collecting and analyzing data, writing reports, and supporting the Project Leader as needed. The role is offered initially on a one-year contract with potential renewal, and applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until March 31, 2026.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Assistant
description: This position offers a full-time or part-time research role supporting a project on privacy law and data protection reforms in Hong Kong. The Research Assistant will conduct literature reviews, assist with data collection and stakeholder interviews, analyze research findings, and help prepare outputs and workshop events. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Law or related field, demonstrate strong legal research and writing skills, and be fluent in English and Cantonese with subject-matter interest. The initial contract runs for at least six months with potential renewal based on performance and funding availability.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position supports a digital humanities initiative developing generative AI models for Chinese calligraphy analysis using a large-scale dataset of historical handwritten documents from the late Qing to early Republican period. The role requires a technical foundation in AI, computer vision, and OCR technologies, combined with at least two years of post-qualification experience, strong communication skills in English and Chinese, and the ability to bridge technical and humanities expertise. Key responsibilities include developing and evaluating two AI models (one for document analysis, one for generative calligraphy), managing dataset updates, conducting user studies, and translating research outputs for public engagement through exhibitions and educational programs. The position offers distinctive opportunities to contribute to cutting-edge AI research while directly impacting museum and classroom applications, working alongside leading researchers and heritage experts.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position supports research into pediatric leukemia genomics, focusing on genetic predisposition and oncogenic mechanisms in childhood acute lymphoblastic leukemia, with particular emphasis on germline GATA3 variants and CRLF2 rearranged B-cell leukemia. The fellow will conduct independent and collaborative research involving large-scale genomic dataset analysis, CRISPR-Cas9 editing, and functional genomics experiments, while also contributing to manuscript preparation, grant proposals, and mentoring. Candidates must hold a PhD in genetics, molecular biology, bioinformatics, or related fields obtained within five years, demonstrate strong publication records in cancer genomics, and possess proficiency in R/Python and genomic analysis pipelines; wet-lab experience is preferred. The two-year renewable contract offers structured mentorship, access to advanced platforms, and multidisciplinary collaboration opportunities with international research partners.

title: Research Associate(s) / Senior Research Assistant(s) (External Fund)
description: The Department of Psychiatry is recruiting Research Associate(s) or Senior Research Assistant(s) to support a large-scale two-phase cohort and clinical trial investigating the relationship between sleep, circadian disturbances, and mental health in adolescents. Candidates must hold a Master's degree (for Research Associates) or Bachelor's degree (for Senior Research Assistants) in Social Sciences, Public Health, or related fields, with at least four years of relevant post-qualification experience, fluency in English and Cantonese, and strong organizational and communication skills. Key responsibilities include overseeing project implementation and timelines, recruiting participants and liaising with schools, mentoring junior staff, executing study protocols, and assisting with data analysis and reporting. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement, with preference given to candidates with youth mental health research experience and statistical analysis knowledge.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in Finance seeks a recent graduate with strong quantitative and coding skills to support research projects spanning environmental, development, and macroeconomic topics under Prof. Henry Zhang's supervision, with secondary oversight from a Wharton faculty member. Essential qualifications include a Bachelor's degree, proficiency in programming (particularly R or Python), familiarity with AI tools, fluency in English, and demonstrated interest in pursuing graduate studies in Economics or Finance, though prior finance coursework is not required and advanced econometrics or statistics knowledge is strongly preferred. Primary responsibilities involve writing R code for data visualization, using APIs to automate PDF data extraction, improving Julia equilibrium solver code, and supporting other research activities as needed. The role is offered on a one-year renewable contract commencing immediately, with application materials including CV, transcript, writing sample, code sample, and two referee contacts submitted through the online portal.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
department: Department of Otorhinolaryngology, Head and Neck Surgery
description: This research assistant position supports AI-driven research projects in otolaryngology, focusing on diagnostics, rhinology, skull base surgery, and olfactory sciences. The successful candidate will hold a bachelor's degree in a relevant field with strong competency in data analysis, programming (Python or R), and AI healthcare applications, alongside excellent analytical and communication skills. Primary responsibilities include data collection and analysis, collaborating with clinical teams to implement AI innovations, contributing to publications, and providing administrative and basic laboratory support. The role offers a one-year renewable contract providing hands-on experience in a multidisciplinary research environment.

title: Junior Research Assistant
description: Junior Research Assistant positions are available in Orthopaedics and Traumatology, based at a hospital in Hong Kong. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences, Pharmacology, or related fields, with demonstrated research experience or clinical research interest, and fluency in both Cantonese and English. The role involves supporting team-based research projects, with project management or clinical research experience being advantageous. Appointments are made on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Executive Officer I (Innovation and Impact Development)
description: This role manages innovation and impact development within a Knowledge Transfer Office, requiring the appointee to lead stakeholder engagement and secure major government and innovation funding (such as RAISe+ and TSSSU), oversee funding programme administration, and provide strategic support to senior committees through report writing and policy analysis. Candidates must have at least 8 years of relevant executive experience with a proven track record in competitive funding acquisition, advanced proficiency in both English and Chinese, and demonstrated expertise in drafting complex funding applications and strategic documents. The position involves directing impact assessments, coordinating flagship events, and managing multiple strategic projects autonomously under tight deadlines, demanding exceptional analytical, communication, and negotiation skills with senior stakeholders. Appointment is on a two-year renewable contract basis, with shortlisted applicants required to complete a written assessment.

title: Research Associate (6-month contract)
department: Office for Greater Bay Area Developments
description: This six-month contract position supports a university office coordinating Greater Bay Area development initiatives, with a focus on organizing a national marine knowledge competition. The Research Associate will manage project execution, coordinate with mainland and Hong Kong stakeholders, assist committees, and provide logistical support for events and daily operations. Candidates must hold a Master's degree with at least four years of relevant experience, demonstrate fluency in English, Cantonese, and Putonghua, and possess strong project coordination skills—preferably with cross-border government collaborations. The role emphasizes interpersonal communication, organizational ability, and proficiency in standard office software.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
description: This Project Co-ordinator II position supports a newly established research centre focused on Electronic Design Automation, requiring the appointee to manage diverse administrative, financial, and personnel tasks across the institute. Key responsibilities include providing executive and secretarial support to committees, managing research project documentation and budgets, coordinating major events and workshops, and overseeing research assistant affairs. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree with at least two years of post-qualification project management experience preferably in tertiary education, demonstrating strong organisational and interpersonal skills alongside fluency in English and Chinese (including Cantonese and Putonghua). The role is offered on an initial two-year renewable contract, with preference given to candidates with solid conference organisation experience.

title: Project Co-ordinator II (Manuscript Editing)
department: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Press
description: This position involves overseeing the complete publication lifecycle of scholarly manuscripts from submission through final proofing, while coordinating with internal teams and external partners including authors, designers, and indexers. The role requires managing administrative responsibilities such as database records, project budgets, file organization, and report preparation. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Humanities or Social Sciences with at least three years of relevant editorial experience, along with fluent English and Chinese proficiency, strong communication skills, familiarity with the Chicago Manual of Style, and experience editing traditional Chinese materials. The appointment is initially for one year and renewable based on performance.

title: Executive Officer I/II (carrying the job title of ‘Senior Manager / Manager’), Office of Taught Postgraduate Programmes
description: This position manages admissions and student services for taught postgraduate programmes within a leading business school's faculty. The successful candidate will supervise a team handling admissions data, student recruitment events, and stakeholder coordination while reporting to the Administrative Director. Key requirements include a bachelor's degree, at least 8 years of executive experience (preferably in higher education), strong data analytics and presentation skills, and deep knowledge of the Chinese Mainland education sector. The role is distinctive for its requirement to balance operational efficiency with high-quality outputs across a large, internationally diverse student population, and demands flexibility including weekend work and business travel.

title: Assistant Computer Officer
department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
description: This role involves establishing and maintaining the department's network infrastructure, Linux servers, and IT systems while developing web applications and providing technical support to staff and students. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field with demonstrated experience in network administration, Linux server management, and web programming, alongside strong problem-solving abilities. Additional expertise in VPN setup, high-performance computing, or Cisco certifications (CCNA/CCNP) would be advantageous. The position is offered initially on a 2-year renewable contract with performance-based continuation.

title: Executive Officer II
description: This Executive Officer II position supports the Graduate Division and Nursing School through executive assistance, postgraduate programme administration, student affairs management, and staff supervision. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree with at least 5 years of post-qualification executive experience, preferably in tertiary academic administration, alongside fluent English and Chinese (including Cantonese and Putonghua). The role requires strong organisational, communication, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work independently under pressure; experience in curriculum management, student affairs, or committee services at universities is advantageous. The appointment is a 2-year contract with gratuity, renewable based on performance, and shortlisted candidates will complete a written assessment.

title: Transport Assistant II(s) (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Transport Operations Officer’)
description: This Transport Assistant II role, officially titled Assistant Transport Operations Officer, involves overseeing daily campus shuttle operations, coordinating bus scheduling and maintenance, managing school bus services, and providing administrative support including data entry, complaint handling, and occasional driving. The position requires secondary education with specific grades in Chinese and English, at least two years of clerical and administrative experience (preferably in tertiary or transport sectors), a valid driving licence for private cars and light goods vehicles, strong bilingual English and Chinese skills, and proficiency in MS Office applications. Key qualities include responsibility, positive attitude, teamwork capability, and knowledge of vehicle maintenance and repair. The role operates on a five-day week schedule with potential outdoor inspections and overtime, offered on an initial two-year renewable contract basis.

title: Project Co-ordinator II(s)
department: The Hong Kong Jockey Club Sports Medicine and Health Sciences Centre
description: The position involves coordinating and executing community sports medicine events, providing secretarial and administrative support to sports medicine associations, and assisting with postgraduate programmes and short courses at a hospital-based centre. Candidates require a bachelor's degree and at least three years of experience in event/project management or administrative support, alongside strong problem-solving, interpersonal, and bilingual communication skills in English and Chinese.
