The role demands self-initiative, the ability to work independently and multitask, and occasional work beyond standard office hours. Appointments are offered initially on a one-year renewable contract.

title: Executive Officer II (carrying the job title of 'Manager, Content and Event'), Alumni Affairs and Development Office
description: This position manages content creation and event execution for a major business school's alumni office, which serves a 47,000-strong global alumni network. The role encompasses full-cycle responsibilities including content production (writing, design, video), digital communications management across CRM and CMS platforms, event planning and logistics, and campaign effectiveness tracking. The appointee will supervise freelance and student contributors, control budgets and timelines, and contribute to alumni engagement strategy development. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree in communication, public relations, advertising, marketing, or related fields, with flexibility for extended hours and business travel required.

title: Clerical Assistant(s) (carrying the job title of ‘Operations Assistant’)
description: This Operations Assistant position in the Transport Office combines clerical administrative work with customer-facing and operational duties. Responsibilities include standard office tasks (filing, data entry), counter service with passenger enquiry and complaint handling, management of campus school bus services and facilities, and occasional driving duties as needed. Candidates must have completed secondary education, strong word processing and computer skills, a valid driving licence for cars and light goods vehicles, fluent written and spoken English and Chinese, and excellent interpersonal and customer service abilities. The role is offered on a two-year renewable contract, with preference given to applicants with prior experience in customer service or transportation sectors.

title: Executive Officer II (carrying the job title of 'Manager, CRM and Digital Engagement'), Alumni Affairs and Development Office
description: This position manages customer relationship management (CRM) systems and digital engagement infrastructure for the Alumni Affairs and Development Office, supporting a 47,000-strong alumni network across 30+ countries. Key responsibilities include overseeing CRM platforms and tools, maintaining data quality and integrity, generating engagement reports, coordinating events, and collaborating with internal teams and external vendors on system improvements and strategy development. The role requires expertise in CRM systems, data management, and event coordination, alongside project management capabilities and the flexibility to work outside standard hours. This is a distinctive opportunity to shape alumni engagement strategy through digital innovation within a leading business school's specialized alumni relations function.

title: Assistant Librarian II (carrying the job title of 'Special Collections Librarian')
department: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Library
description: This position involves managing Special Collections focused on Chinese and Hong Kong studies, encompassing materials ranging from ancient oracle bones to contemporary literary archives. The librarian will report to the Head of Special Collections and undertake acquisition support including donor relations, faculty liaison services, exhibition development, outreach coordination, and helpdesk duties. The role requires a postgraduate qualification in librarianship or information science, three years of relevant professional experience, and demonstrated expertise in collection management, preservation, archival standards (DACS, EAD, MARC), and archives management systems such as ArchivesSpace. Candidates should possess supervisory abilities, strong IT skills, fluency in Chinese and English, and proven capacity for collaborative work across the institution; an advanced degree in related disciplines such as China Studies or History is advantageous.

title: Assistant Librarian II (carrying the job title of ‘Digital Transformation Services Librarian’)
department: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Library
description: This position seeks a technically skilled librarian to lead digital transformation initiatives at an academic library, reporting to the Head of Digital Initiatives. Primary responsibilities include driving digital service improvements, implementing emerging technologies, developing web and software applications across their full lifecycle, managing digitisation and digital repository operations, and fostering change management and innovation culture across the institution. The ideal candidate holds a university degree in computer science or software engineering combined with a postgraduate qualification in librarianship, has at least three years of relevant professional experience, and demonstrates expertise in web application development, Python programming, Linux/Windows systems, and emerging technologies such as text-mining and computer vision. The role uniquely bridges technical software development with library science, requiring the appointee to balance both developer and support responsibilities while managing complex digital projects and shift duties at library helpdesks.

title: Project Co-ordinator II(s) (carrying the job title of ‘Executive, Corporate Communications’)
description: The Communications and Public Relations Office is recruiting Project Co-ordinators II to support the university's strategic communication objectives across media relations, event management, and content creation. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree (preferably in Communications or Journalism) with at least 5 years of experience in public relations or corporate communications, alongside fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua). Key responsibilities include planning and executing press events, managing media enquiries, producing bilingual publicity materials, and maintaining relationships with media outlets. The role is distinctive for offering hands-on exposure across diverse communication areas with opportunities for professional development, initially offered on a 2-year renewable contract.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant (The Hong Kong Genome Project)
description: This position supports a government-backed genomic medicine initiative, requiring a Bachelor's degree in genetics, health sciences, biomedical science, or related fields, with at least two years of relevant experience. The role involves conducting basic research, managing project correspondence, assisting genetic counsellors, recruiting and interviewing study participants in clinical settings, and handling administrative duties. Candidates should be detail-oriented team players with strong English and Chinese language skills, though experience in patient care, clinical research, or digital health technologies is advantageous. The appointment is initially contract-based for one year, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate (carrying the job title of “Project Manager”)
description: The Department of Obstetrics and Gynaecology seeks a Research Associate/Project Manager to establish and manage high-quality prospective clinical studies. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in Biology, Biochemistry, Life Science or related field, with at least four years of clinical research experience, fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua), and strong interpersonal skills. Key responsibilities include developing management systems, securing regulatory approvals, coordinating study documentation, training research sites, managing budgets, monitoring protocol compliance, supervising data collection and validation, and reporting progress to oversight committees and funders. The position is offered initially on a one-year fixed contract, renewable based on performance.

title: Assistant Director of Human Resources (at the rank of Senior Assistant Secretary II)
description: This senior human resources role involves leading a team within an established HR department to oversee recruitment, staff engagement, performance management, benefits, and learning and talent development functions across the organization. The appointee will be responsible for developing and implementing HR policies, driving talent development strategies such as performance management enhancement and succession planning frameworks, and supporting workforce planning in partnership with academic and administrative units. Candidates must hold a good honours degree, demonstrate fluency in English and Chinese including Putonghua, possess substantial HR or public administration experience (preferably senior-level in higher education), and have strong knowledge of employment law, communication skills, and analytical abilities. The position is offered initially on a three-year contract basis renewable by mutual agreement, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written assessment.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This postdoctoral position focuses on lung cancer research, including genomics, tumor microenvironment investigation, and therapeutic development. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Biological or Chemical Sciences with molecular biology experience, or a Ph.D. in Computer Science with bioinformatics expertise, along with demonstrated commitment to research. The fellow will conduct independent research, publish findings, apply for grants, and mentor junior researchers at a hospital research facility. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
description: This part-time research assistant position requires a Bachelor's degree in a health-related field (preferably Nursing) plus two to three years of relevant work experience, with demonstrated capability in conducting and coordinating research studies using both qualitative and quantitative methods. Key responsibilities include assisting with data collection, implementing intervention protocols, performing literature reviews, managing data, and writing reports, while maintaining proficiency in English and Chinese and competence with MS Office and SPSS. The role demands independent work ability and offers a one-year renewable contract commencing immediately, with candidates required to submit their CV, qualification certificates, and supporting documents through the online application portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This postdoctoral or research associate position supports breast cancer research, requiring either a PhD or a Master's degree with five years of cancer research experience. The successful candidate will conduct experimental work in cell biology, animal studies, and molecular techniques while collaborating with postgraduate students and supporting the writing of manuscripts and grant proposals at a hospital-based research facility. Key competencies include solid technical expertise in molecular biology, strong research motivation, and data management skills, with next-generation sequencing and bioinformatics experience being advantageous. The initial appointment is for one year on a contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This position is a Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant in pathology conducting cancer research across multiple sites. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Biomedical Sciences or related fields, with practical experience in animal handling, tissue culture, and molecular analytical techniques. The role involves collaborative research with department staff and offers an initial one-year contract renewable by mutual agreement, making it suitable for recent graduates seeking postgraduate experience. Required application materials include a CV detailing academic qualifications and research experience, academic certificates, and two reference letters.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant (carrying the job title of ‘Project Officer/Assistant Project Officer’)
description: This Research Assistant position in Electronic Engineering requires a Bachelor's degree and preferably two years of tertiary experience, with strong bilingual English-Chinese communication skills and proficiency in MS Office and graphic design. The role involves assisting with program administration, project management, and supporting the organization of postgraduate events and activities. Applicants must demonstrate responsibility, self-motivation, and independent working capability; fresh graduates are eligible. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract, with applications submitted online via cover letter and CV.

title: Assistant Human Resources Officer
description: This General Clerk II position in the Human Resources Office supports personnel administration, staff leave and benefits management, correspondence handling, and general office operations. Applicants must have secondary education completion with specified HKCEE or HKDSE qualifications in Chinese and English, strong word-processing and computer skills (MS Office proficiency), and fluent written and spoken English and Cantonese. While HR sector experience of two years or more is advantageous, candidates with general administrative backgrounds are welcome, and the role offers contract appointment for up to two years with potential progression to Human Resources Officer for degree-qualified candidates demonstrating career advancement potential.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The Department of Psychiatry seeks Research Assistant(s) or Junior Research Assistant(s) to support a large-scale cohort and clinical trial study investigating the relationship between sleep disturbances, circadian disruption, and mental health outcomes in adolescents through a two-phase research design. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Social Sciences, Public Health, or related fields, be bilingual in English and Cantonese, and demonstrate strong organisational and communication skills with genuine enthusiasm for research. Primary responsibilities include conducting clinical and research interviews, performing neurocognitive and sleep assessments, assisting with psychological intervention development, and supporting data entry and analysis; on-the-job training is provided. The position is offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) – Cross Cultural Analytics (Data Science, NLP, Computational Linguistics, Computational Humanities)
description: Two full-time postdoctoral fellows are sought to join an interdisciplinary data science project examining the historical influence of Chinese culture in Europe and North America (1550–1900) using large-scale text data, NLP, knowledge graphs, and AI-driven methods. Applicants must hold a PhD in Computer Science, NLP, Computational Linguistics, Data Science, or a related technical field, with strong Python programming skills and demonstrated experience working with textual data, modern NLP tools, and machine learning frameworks. The role involves developing and applying NLP methods (including OCR correction, semantic search, and knowledge-graph modelling), processing large multilingual historical corpora, co-authoring research papers for digital humanities and computational linguistics venues, and collaborating with an international research team. Preference will be given to candidates with experience in multilingual NLP, digital text pipelines, graph neural networks, or interest in cultural analytics and historical text analysis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: This position invites Postdoctoral Fellows and Research Assistants to join a multidisciplinary orthopaedic research team developing AI-enabled and augmented reality surgical technologies, including AR navigation systems, AI diagnostic platforms, and surgical planning tools. Postdoctoral candidates require a PhD in Biomedical Engineering, Computer Science, or related fields with demonstrated expertise in grant writing, publications, and project management, while Research Assistants need a Bachelor's or Master's degree with strong research interest and capability in software testing or data annotation. Key responsibilities include preparing grant proposals and manuscripts, coordinating with collaborators, supporting software development and clinical data handling, and tracking project milestones. Appointments are initially one-year contracts renewable based on performance, with applications requiring a detailed CV, academic certificates, and two reference letters submitted through the online career portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate position in the School of Journalism and Communication seeks a researcher to lead and support work in corporate social responsibility and sustainability communication. The ideal candidate holds a PhD (or Master's degree for the Research Associate track) in Communication or a related field, with at least four years of post-qualification experience and demonstrated expertise in both quantitative and qualitative research methods. Primary responsibilities include developing research protocols, managing data collection and analysis, publishing findings, and coordinating research activities across CSR and sustainability topics. The position offers a one-year renewable contract, with preference given to candidates with interdisciplinary research backgrounds and computational or data-driven research experience.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: The Department of Surgery is recruiting Research Assistant(s) to support machine learning research focused on improving diagnosis and clinical workflows for cardio-thoracic conditions, particularly thoracic aortic aneurysms and dissections. Candidates must hold at least a Bachelor's degree in healthcare, biomedical engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, or related fields, with demonstrated programming expertise and hands-on experience developing machine learning systems; knowledge of cardiovascular anatomy and medical imaging modalities is essential. Primary responsibilities include designing and optimizing machine learning systems to enhance TAAD diagnosis and standardize clinical reporting, alongside administrative research duties. The position is offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in the Department of Finance seeks candidates with a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or related fields, along with strong programming proficiency in R, MATLAB, and/or Python. The role involves assisting with actuarial science research and performing assigned duties, with preference given to applicants with prior research experience in actuarial science or statistics. The position is initially offered on a six-month renewable contract, contingent on performance and mutual agreement, and requires applicants to demonstrate their programming capabilities and research background in their application materials.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in nursing requires a Bachelor's degree plus at least two years of relevant experience in nursing, public health, or related disciplines, with demonstrated expertise in quantitative and qualitative research, grant writing, and manuscript preparation. Key responsibilities include initiating and conducting research projects, analyzing data, drafting grant proposals and academic publications, and supporting the School Research Committee administratively. Proficiency in both English and Chinese (written and spoken) is essential. The role is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis and requires submission of a CV and academic certificates demonstrating language proficiency and qualifications.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant (carrying the job title of ‘Project Officer/Assistant Project Officer’)
department: Department of Mechanical and Automation Engineering
description: This position offers a research support role within a mechanical engineering department, carrying the title of Project Officer or Assistant Project Officer depending on experience level. Applicants require a bachelor's degree and preferably 2-3 years of tertiary institution experience, along with strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, excellent communication and organizational skills, and proficiency with standard office software and Chinese word processing. The role involves assisting with office administration, project management, and coordinating postgraduate programme events and activities. The appointment is offered on a two-year renewable contract basis, with prior postgraduate programme administration experience valued but not essential.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This role involves supporting sleep and mental health research studies through participant recruitment, conducting clinical and research interviews, data entry and analysis, and other assigned tasks. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Psychology, Counselling, Medical Sciences, Social Work, or related fields, be proficient Cantonese speakers with strong communication skills, and demonstrate organization, reliability, and enthusiasm for research and clinical translation in mental and sleep health. Prior experience in psychological or psychiatric research is preferred, and the position requires occasional work outside standard hours and weekends. The appointment is initially a three-month contract, renewable by mutual agreement, with on-the-job training provided.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Statistics and Data Science
description: This research assistant position is based at a leading equine doping control laboratory while employed by the university's Department of Statistics and Data Science. The role focuses on discovering and validating biomarkers for equine doping detection through proteomic and transcriptomic analysis, requiring collaboration with biochemists, analysts, bioinformaticians, and statisticians. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree in molecular biology, biochemistry, or related field, with strong English and Chinese communication skills, excellent organizational abilities, and preferably hands-on experience with PCR, next-generation sequencing, or liquid chromatography/mass spectrometry. The position involves laboratory work including specimen analysis, instrument maintenance, and ensuring compliance with international quality assurance standards.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: Postdoctoral fellows are sought to conduct nursing and health research, developing new projects, managing ongoing studies, and contributing to grant writing and academic publications. Candidates must hold a PhD in nursing, public health, health-related fields, or social sciences, with demonstrated expertise in quantitative or qualitative research, strong publication records, and fluency in both English and Chinese. The position offers an initial one-year contract, renewable based on performance and agreement. Applicants should apply online through the designated university careers portal.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The position involves supporting research projects in molecular diagnostics and circulating DNA at two facilities, with responsibilities including assisting with molecular testing, data analysis, sample processing, and routine laboratory operations using techniques such as PCR and DNA sequencing. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Medical Laboratory Science, Biomedical Science, Life Science, or related fields, with preferred laboratory experience and fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese (Cantonese and Mandarin). The role requires self-motivation, responsibility, and strong teamwork abilities. The appointment is initially for one year on a contract basis, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position is based at a hospital facility and supports public health research focused on behavioral and mental health promotion across Hong Kong and mainland China. The role involves coordinating research activities, leading group-based health promotion projects, and conducting data analysis, with on-the-job training provided in research methodology, biostatistics, and epidemiology. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in public health, biostatistics, epidemiology, psychology, or related fields, with statistical skills and fluency in English and Cantonese; prior research coordination or intervention project experience is advantageous. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The Department of Orthopaedics and Traumatology is recruiting Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, or Research Assistants to conduct basic research on musculoskeletal tissue repair, particularly investigating therapeutics for tissue repair and identifying novel markers of tendinopathy using transcriptomics and lipidomics approaches. Candidates should hold a PhD in Bioinformatics, Biomedical Sciences, Bioengineering, or Biology with strong research skills in molecular and cell biology techniques, solid publication records, and the ability to work independently; those with MPhil or BSc qualifications plus relevant experience will be considered for lower-level appointments. Key responsibilities include experimental research on tissue repair, biomarker discovery, grant writing, and laboratory management. Positions are offered on a one-year renewable contract, and successful appointees may pursue their own PhD research concurrently with supervisor approval.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position supports quantitative marketing research projects and requires a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Economics, Statistics, Business Analytics, or related fields, alongside strong programming skills in Excel, R, and/or Python and excellent English proficiency. Key responsibilities include organizing data collection, conducting literature reviews, managing data for case studies, and providing administrative research support. The role demands meticulous attention to detail, methodical work practices, reliability in teamwork, and the ability to execute precise instructions consistently over extended periods, with prior experience in quantitative research preferred. The position is offered on a six-month contract basis, renewable based on performance, commencing as soon as possible.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in the Department of Surgery requires a Master's degree in Biochemistry, Biological Sciences, or a related field, along with demonstrated expertise in mammalian cell culture and molecular techniques such as DNA/RNA extraction, RT-PCR, cytotoxicity assays, and immunofluorescence imaging. The role involves working directly with clinical specimens, including medical sample collection and processing of patient-derived organoids, making it distinctive for its clinical research focus. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement, commencing as soon as possible.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Mechanical and Automation Engineering
description: The department seeks research staff at postdoctoral, associate, or assistant levels to develop an integrated UAV system and digital traffic platform combining robotics, artificial intelligence, and traffic modeling. Postdoctoral Fellows and Research Associates will conduct advanced research on UAV optimization, control algorithms, and traffic prediction systems, while Research Assistants will provide support through data collection, field testing, and model training. Candidates require a PhD in relevant disciplines (postdocs), a Master's degree with four years' experience (associates), or a Bachelor's degree (assistants) in Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Traffic Engineering, or related fields. Positions are contract-based for up to two years beginning March 2026, with renewal possible based on performance.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s) (External Fund)
department: The Jockey Club School of Public Health and Primary Care
description: The school is recruiting Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, and Research Assistants to conduct health research using large administrative datasets. Candidates must hold a PhD in public health, epidemiology, biostatistics, or related fields (for postdoctoral roles), or a Master's degree with relevant experience, and demonstrate proficiency in statistical software and quantitative analysis. Responsibilities include developing research projects, managing datasets, writing proposals and publications, and assisting the Principal Investigator. Positions are initially contracted for one year with the possibility of renewal based on performance.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: Applicants with a Master's degree (Research Assistant) or Bachelor's/Diploma (Junior Research Assistant) in biological or medical sciences are sought to support microbiology research. Primary responsibilities include managing bacterial strains and stocks, ordering laboratory supplies, conducting laboratory tests, and performing experiments with associated data collection and reporting. The role offers hands-on experience in microbiology, biochemistry, and molecular biology, with work split between a hospital setting and a science park. The initial one-year contract position is renewable based on mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: The Jockey Club School of Public Health and Primary Care
description: This position offers research support roles at two levels: Research Assistant (Master's degree required) or Junior Research Assistant (Bachelor's degree required) in a public health setting. Candidates must demonstrate proficiency in statistical software (SPSS, STATA, SAS, R, or NVIVO), strong bilingual English-Chinese communication skills, and the ability to conduct either quantitative statistical analysis or qualitative thematic analysis. Key responsibilities include conducting literature reviews and data extraction, managing research projects using qualitative and quantitative methods, analysing data, and preparing reports. The role is initially contracted for up to six months with potential renewal, and prior experience in evidence-based medicine, guideline development, or traditional Chinese medicine is valued.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral fellowship in finance requires a Ph.D. in Actuarial Science or related field, with demonstrated research excellence, strong English proficiency, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. The fellow will conduct high-quality actuarial research, develop research proposals, write articles and reports, and present findings under faculty supervision. The one-year contract position begins in January 2026 and requires submission of a cover letter, CV with complete publication list, research proposal, two recent publications, and three referee contacts through the online application portal.

title: Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: This Research Assistant position in Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences involves conducting ophthalmic investigations, extracting medical data, and coordinating patient follow-ups across clinical and translational research projects covering diverse eye diseases and conditions. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree, demonstrate proficiency in English and Cantonese, show genuine interest in medical research, and possess strong interpersonal skills; experience with statistical analysis software and clinical research is advantageous but not required. The role offers opportunities to contribute to landmark publications, develop specialized ophthalmological expertise using advanced instruments, and pursue postgraduate study, with appointments initially made on a one-year renewable contract basis. Fresh graduates are welcome to apply, and those without a Bachelor's degree may be considered for a Junior Research Assistant position.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
department: School of Hotel and Tourism Management
description: The School of Hotel and Tourism Management seeks Postdoctoral Fellow(s) to conduct independent and collaborative research in real estate economics, finance, and related fields including urban and environmental economics. Applicants must hold or be near completion of a PhD in real estate, finance, economics, public policy, or related disciplines, with strong programming skills (STATA, SAS, Python, R) and solid knowledge of econometrics and mathematical modeling. Fellows will be responsible for producing high-quality research suitable for top-tier journals and contributing to the School's intellectual and research culture while working alongside faculty experts in real estate finance, behavioral finance, and monetary policy. The position offers a one-year renewable contract starting as soon as possible or in August 2026.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Geography and Resource Management
description: This postdoctoral position involves conducting original, high-quality research under the supervision of Prof. Jack Harris in areas such as evolutionary economic geography, sustainable transitions, Green FinTech, and entrepreneurial ecosystems. Candidates must hold a PhD in Human Geography or related disciplines, demonstrate a strong publication record, possess excellent English proficiency, and show capability for both independent and collaborative work. The role is offered on an 18-month contract basis, with the start date flexible until March 1st, 2026. Applicants should submit a cover letter, CV, two-page research plan, and three referee contacts through the online application portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position in a pharmacy research team involves leading a Chinese medicine-funded project with responsibilities spanning research logistics, fieldwork oversight, data management, and mentoring of junior staff. Essential qualifications include a PhD in a healthcare or scientific discipline, strong statistical software proficiency, fluency in Cantonese and English, and proven independent research capability, with preference given to candidates with experience in artificial intelligence, project management, or implementation science. The role requires coordinating multi-site studies, developing educational materials, ensuring data quality across collection through analysis, and organizing training sessions. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate
description: This Research Associate position supports a Chinese Medicine Development Fund project within the School of Pharmacy, focusing on acquiring and analyzing global pharmaceutical data using web crawling, deep learning, and large language models. The ideal candidate holds a Ph.D. in applied mathematics, engineering, statistics, pharmaceutical sciences, or related life sciences, with proven programming skills in languages such as Python or Java and preferably background knowledge in pharmacology, pharmacometrics, or systems pharmacology. Key responsibilities include developing and maintaining data acquisition platforms, classifying pharmaceutical information using advanced computational techniques, creating project interfaces, and monitoring overall project progress while working collaboratively within a research team. The role is initially contracted for one year with renewal possibilities, requiring strong independent work capabilities, excellent organizational skills, and fluent English communication.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position supports a Chinese Medicine Development Fund project within a pharmacy school, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Pharmacy or related field and strong proficiency in English-language scientific literature, data management, and office productivity tools. The role involves systematically searching, collecting, organizing, and synthesizing pharmaceutical materials and databases; extracting and standardizing key data points; and maintaining accurate records to support the research team's work. The successful candidate must demonstrate meticulous attention to detail, analytical skills, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively under tight deadlines. The position is offered initially on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral or research associate position in nursing requires either a PhD in nursing or a health-related field, or a Master's degree with four years of post-qualification research experience. The role focuses on initiating and developing research projects, conducting empirical studies, analyzing data, and supporting the preparation of grant proposals and academic publications. Candidates must demonstrate strong quantitative and/or qualitative research capabilities with an established publication record, along with fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese including Cantonese. The position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: This Research Associate/Research Assistant position supports a top-ranked finance department and its Finance for Society Research Centre, which bridges academic research with industry practitioners across areas including quantitative trading, sustainable finance, behavioral finance, and fintech. The role involves providing research support, administrative coordination, and liaison work for both the department and research centre, reporting to the Department Chairperson. Candidates must hold a PhD in Finance, Economics, or Business (or a Master's degree for the Assistant track), with strong data analysis and policy research skills, fluency in English and Chinese, and demonstrated ability to work independently and collaboratively. The position is offered on a two-year renewable contract basis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral or research associate position supports a major research centre specializing in Chinese music and opera, which maintains extensive archival collections and organizes public engagement activities. The successful candidate will manage funding applications, donor relations, research office support, and impact documentation while assisting with public and media engagement initiatives. Applicants must hold a PhD in Music, Chinese Studies, or related fields (or a Master's with four years' relevant experience), demonstrate fluency in English and Chinese, and possess professional experience in grant writing, event management, and archival work. The role offers a two-year renewable contract requiring both independent work and close collaboration with faculty on research planning and dissemination activities.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The position supports preclinical PET/MR imaging research at a newly established neuroscience research institute, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Medical Imaging, Radiography, or a related field. Responsibilities include daily facility operations such as equipment maintenance and calibration, coordinating compliance with campus and animal care services, managing facility bookings and procurement, and ensuring ethical standards for animal research. The ideal candidate should have strong organizational and communication skills, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, familiarity with MRI technology, and preferably prior experience in laboratory research and animal handling. The role is offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable subject to satisfactory performance.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Mechanical and Automation Engineering
description: This Research Assistant position supports cutting-edge development of autonomous, learning-based robotic systems for surgical automation, with emphasis on multi-arm manipulation and embodied AI using platforms like the dVRK system. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Robotics, or Engineering, with demonstrated knowledge of imitation and reinforcement learning, hands-on experience using robotics simulators and deep learning frameworks, and strong problem-solving abilities. Key responsibilities include building realistic surgical simulation environments in Isaac-SIM, implementing state-of-the-art learning algorithms for surgical tasks, transferring trained policies from simulation to physical robots, and supporting real-world experiments with robotic hardware. The role offers valuable experience at the intersection of robotics, AI, and medicine, with preference given to candidates with prior experience in Isaac-SIM, ROS/ROS2 multi-robot control, simulation, and physical robotic systems.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Assistant
description: This full-time or part-time position supports research administration within a nursing school, focusing on healthy lifestyle and preventive medicine initiatives. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as information technology, health, or social science, with at least two years of research experience in a tertiary institution. Key responsibilities include providing administrative support for research matters, assisting with grant applications, organizing research activities, and liaising between academic and external parties. Essential qualifications include fluency in both English and Chinese, strong IT competency, excellent coordination skills, and the ability to work independently with minimal supervision. The role is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: Research assistants are sought to support health-risk behaviour research projects, requiring a bachelor's degree or higher in disciplines such as statistics, psychology, social sciences, or public health, along with fluency in English, Chinese, and Cantonese, strong computer literacy, and excellent organisational and communication skills. Primary responsibilities include implementing interventions, recruiting participants and collecting data, conducting literature reviews, managing data entry, and preparing reports and publications. Candidates must demonstrate self-motivation, responsibility, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. Positions are offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks motivated Research or Junior Research Assistants to support clinical and translational research programmes across diverse eye conditions including glaucoma, retinal diseases, dry eye disease, and ocular cancers. Key responsibilities involve conducting ophthalmic investigations, managing medical data, and coordinating participant follow-ups, with applicants requiring a Bachelor's degree (or relevant experience for Junior positions), fluency in English and Cantonese, genuine interest in ophthalmology research, and a patient-centred approach. Preferred qualifications include prior clinical or laboratory research experience and proficiency in statistical analysis software; fresh graduates are encouraged to apply. The role offers opportunities to participate in landmark research projects, develop specialised investigative skills with advanced instruments, and pursue career advancement and postgraduate studies, with initial one-year renewable contracts commencing immediately.

title: Research Assistant
department: Institute for Tissue Engineering and Regenerative Medicine
description: This Research Assistant position supports tissue engineering and regenerative medicine research, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences or related field (Master's preferred) and practical expertise in primary cell culture, histology, immunohistochemistry, and data analysis. The role involves conducting research under supervisor guidance, assisting laboratory personnel with technical training, and performing assigned laboratory duties, with on-the-job training provided to develop specialized skills. Candidates must be self-motivated, well-organized, independent workers with strong communication abilities in English and Chinese. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral position in the School of Biomedical Sciences seeks a PhD-holder with expertise in stem cell engineering, sequencing/gene editing technology, or regenerative biology for nerve injury research. The successful candidate will support the Principal Investigator in project management, establish and maintain research platforms, supervise junior team members, and contribute to manuscript and grant preparation. Applicants must demonstrate strong publication records, bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively under pressure. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: This Research Assistant position in Orthopaedics and Traumatology focuses on advancing AI technology and optimizing large language models for medical applications using Python, R, PyTorch, and TensorFlow. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Biomedical Science, Computer Science, or related fields, with demonstrated expertise in unsupervised deep learning, statistical programming, and proficiency in both English and Chinese. Key responsibilities include conducting clinical trials, analyzing data, manipulating medical images, and implementing deep learning solutions, with additional requirements for JavaScript and Vue.js framework familiarity. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract starting immediately.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
description: This part-time Research Assistant position in the School of Pharmacy supports Prof. Joyce You's research team, primarily conducting health economics research involving retrospective data collection and analysis of medical costs, literature reviews, and decision-analytic modeling. Candidates must hold at least a Bachelor's degree in Health Economics or related fields (Master's preferred) and demonstrate bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English alongside strong communication skills. The role encompasses database management, Delphi surveys, and various research support tasks assigned by the principal investigator. Applications require submission of a CV and relevant certificates through the online application portal.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This position seeks a Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant in the School of Biomedical Sciences to conduct research on nerve injury and repair projects while coordinating laboratory operations. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Medical or Biomedical Sciences and demonstrate at least three years of technical experience in animal studies, particularly microsurgical techniques on rodent, rabbit, or pig models. Additional qualifications include proficiency in tissue sectioning, immunohistochemistry, neuro-electrophysiology, and related techniques, along with strong self-motivation and responsibility. The role is initially offered on a one-year renewable contract contingent on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
department: Department of Geography and Resource Management
description: Full-time postdoctoral positions are available to conduct research on urban land use change modelling, climate change adaptation, smart cities, or urban informatics in China and the Greater Bay Area. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Human Geography, Urban Studies, GIS, or related fields, with demonstrated expertise in spatial data analysis, GIS, R, Python, and statistical software, along with strong English proficiency and a track record of quality publications. The role involves conducting independent research, preparing proposals and articles, and presenting findings, with preferred experience in cellular automata modelling, agent-based modelling, or GeoAI. The initial appointment is for one year commencing July 2025, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: A Research Associate or Research Assistant position is available in a biomedical sciences research group focusing on cellular studies and functional characterization of disease-causing genes. Applicants must hold a Master's degree or above in biomedical research or a related field; Research Associates additionally require at least four years of post-qualification experience. Experience in stem cell culture and CRISPR/Cas9 genome editing is particularly valued. The role is offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance, and candidates should submit a curriculum vitae and reference letter through the online application portal.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in the School of Life Sciences involves participating in a butterfly-related research project, with responsibilities including conducting field and molecular biology experiments, drafting reports, collaborating with team members, and providing administrative support. Applicants require a Bachelor's degree or higher in Biology or a related field, bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English, and demonstrated self-motivation and organizational skills, though laboratory experience is advantageous and fresh graduates are encouraged to apply. The role is initially contracted for one year with potential renewal, and on-the-job training will be provided. Applications must be submitted online through the institutional career portal.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks full-time or part-time research assistants to support clinical and research programmes focused on eye diseases including glaucoma, diabetic retinopathy, and dry eye disease, with particular emphasis on Chinese medicine treatments. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree in Chinese Medicine, Medicine, Public Health, Statistics, or related life sciences, demonstrate proficiency in English and Cantonese, and possess strong organisational and analytical skills; registered Chinese Medicine Practitioners with relevant clinical or laboratory experience are preferred. Responsibilities include assisting with clinical studies (literature reviews, data collection and analysis), laboratory work (specimen handling), and related duties, with potential shift work required. Positions are offered on a renewable one to two-year contract basis, with on-the-job training provided.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s)
description: The position offers postdoctoral or research associate roles within a neuroscience laboratory focusing on RNA regulation of brain circuit formation, maintenance, and plasticity using advanced techniques including 2-photon optogenetics, imaging, and electrophysiology. Candidates must hold a PhD in life sciences with demonstrated research excellence, hands-on experience in electrophysiology or advanced imaging, and strong communication skills; molecular biology, bioinformatics, and microscopy expertise are particularly valued. Primary responsibilities include conducting experiments and analysis, presenting findings at conferences, manuscript writing, preparing research documents, and mentoring junior team members in a collaborative environment. The role emphasizes both cutting-edge neuroscience research and professional development support within a top-ranked institution.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: This role supports a neuroscience research laboratory investigating RNA regulation in brain circuit formation and plasticity using advanced techniques including two-photon imaging, optogenetics, and electrophysiology. Responsibilities encompass laboratory management (solutions preparation, animal colony care, equipment maintenance, budgeting) and research support tasks such as assisting with documentation and project reports. Applicants must hold a bachelor's degree in life sciences with prior research experience, demonstrate strong communication and organizational skills, and be proficient in English and Chinese, with particular advantages for candidates with expertise in molecular biology, neuroscience, electrophysiology, or microscopy. The position offers mentorship and career development within a collaborative research environment.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The Department of Electronic Engineering seeks Postdoctoral Fellow(s) and Research Assistant(s) to join an interdisciplinary team developing innovative smart window technology designed for durability and commercial scaling. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in Chemical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, Materials Engineering, or related fields, with a strong research record, independent work capability, and excellent communication skills; experience in smart materials development, light/heat management, or industrial applications is preferred. Responsibilities include assisting in smart window design and development, structural optimization, prototype fabrication and testing, conducting experiments, and collaborating with research staff and students. The initial one-year contract position is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This position supports plant-environment interaction research within a center focused on sustainable agriculture and food security. The Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant will conduct laboratory analyses, molecular experiments, and data collection while maintaining experimental records and cultivating plant materials. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Chemistry, Molecular Biology, Plant Biology, or related field, with strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and ability to work both independently and collaboratively; prior laboratory experience is advantageous. Key responsibilities include assisting with research project design and execution, interpreting experimental data, and staying current with relevant scientific literature while contributing to team discussions.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Assistant
department: Institute for Tissue Engineering and Regenerative Medicine
description: This posting seeks a Research Assistant or Postdoctoral Fellow to join a laboratory investigating tendon and bone tissue engineering, with focus areas including extracellular matrix-based biomaterials, stem cell and exosome therapies, and biomaterial development. Research Assistants require a Bachelor's degree in biomedical science or related field with strong organizational and communication skills, while Postdoctoral Fellows must hold a Doctoral degree and demonstrate independence and self-motivation. Core responsibilities include conducting research experiments, managing laboratory operations, and mentoring staff members. Initial appointments are contract-based for one year, renewable by mutual agreement, with applications requiring a CV, academic certificates, and referee contacts submitted through the online career portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: The Department of Biomedical Engineering seeks postdoctoral fellows to conduct interdisciplinary research on brain structure and function, working with a faculty mentor on data collection, analysis, and model development involving neuroimaging and computational approaches. Candidates must hold a PhD in biomedical engineering or related fields and demonstrate strong mathematical foundations (linear algebra, calculus, optimization, probability, statistics) and programming proficiency (MATLAB, Python), with experience in neuroimaging data (MRI, fMRI, EEG) and machine learning/AI valued highly. The position offers a one-year renewable contract starting immediately, requiring submission of a CV with publications and a brief statement of interest demonstrating alignment with the role's technical requirements.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associates(s)
description: The Department of Electronic Engineering seeks Postdoctoral Fellow(s) or Research Associate(s) to work on deep learning and/or remote sensing projects, specifically focusing on building high-definition maps of Hong Kong using LiDAR, surround-view images, and GNSS data. Candidates must hold a PhD in Computer Science, Electronic Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or a related field, with demonstrated experience in research project development, manuscript preparation, data collection and analysis, and proficiency in written and spoken English. Primary responsibilities include initiating and conducting research projects, collecting and analyzing data, assisting with academic publications and reports, and performing tasks assigned by the Principal Investigator. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis, with the requirement to submit a curriculum vitae, publication record, and academic certificates during application.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: These research positions in electronic engineering seek candidates with a Bachelor's degree to assist with developing deep learning models, managing projects, and collecting/analyzing data within the department. Ideal applicants will demonstrate strong responsibility and attention to detail, analytical and interpersonal skills, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. Key responsibilities include training deep learning models, writing research reports, coordinating with collaborating parties, and performing additional assigned duties. The role is initially offered on a one-year renewable contract, with relevant work experience considered advantageous.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Senior Research Assistant(s) (External Fund) / Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Sports Science and Physical Education
description: The Department of Sports Science and Physical Education seeks multiple research staff at varying levels (Postdoctoral Fellow through Research Assistant) to support externally-funded projects. Candidates must hold relevant qualifications ranging from a PhD (for Postdoctoral Fellow) to a Bachelor's degree (for Research Assistant), with progressively greater experience requirements at higher levels, and must be fluent in English and Chinese. Key responsibilities include liaising with schools and community organisations, planning and delivering project activities, conducting impact assessments, managing vendor relationships, analysing data, and supervising junior staff. Positions are offered on one-year renewable contracts, with experience working with persons with disabilities or special needs considered advantageous.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s)/ Research Assistant(s)
description: Positions are available for Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, and Research Assistants within the Department of Information Engineering's advanced wireless networks laboratory, focusing on wireless networks, stochastic control, and AI/ML applications. Postdoctoral and Associate-level candidates must hold a PhD in a relevant discipline with a demonstrated research track record, while Assistant-level candidates require a Bachelor's degree with strong academic performance and related research experience; hands-on experience with 5G/WiFi systems is advantageous. Appointees will conduct research in the specified areas, with senior-level staff also supervising graduate and undergraduate students. Initial contracts are for one year and renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: The Department of Psychiatry seeks a Research Associate or Research Assistant to support computational neuroscience projects that translate systems neuroscience insights into clinical applications, leveraging brain-wide neural datasets from an international collaboration to study circuit-based neuropathological conditions. Candidates must hold a Master's degree (or Bachelor's for the Assistant role) in computational neuroscience or quantitative disciplines with relevant work experience, demonstrate strong programming skills for analyzing neural and behavioral data, and possess excellent communication abilities and self-motivation. Key responsibilities include analyzing and modeling biological data, developing analytical methodologies, disseminating findings, and collaborating across research teams, with advantages given to candidates with expertise in applied statistics, Python, and machine learning. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract with opportunities for training, international exposure, and publication.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The Gerald Choa Neuroscience Institute seeks postdoctoral fellows, research associates, or research assistants to conduct auditory neuroscience research, including studies on auditory perception, processing, pathology, and cochlear implants. Candidates must hold a PhD in neuroscience or related fields, demonstrate proficiency in Python or MATLAB, possess strong data analysis and signal processing skills, and be willing to work with both human participants and animal models. Key responsibilities include conducting experiments, supporting research design and analysis, collaborating with team members, and mentoring junior researchers. The position offers access to state-of-the-art facilities and opportunities for professional development within a collaborative research environment.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The School of Architecture seeks Research Assistant(s) or Junior Research Assistant(s) to support architectural and multidisciplinary research projects in Hong Kong. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree or higher in Architecture, Landscape Architecture, Design, or related fields, demonstrate proficiency in both written English and Chinese, and possess technical competency in design software (AutoCAD, Photoshop, InDesign, SketchUp) and Microsoft Office applications. Responsibilities include project execution and administration, architectural research and design production, report preparation, fieldwork coordination, and collaborative engagement across interdisciplinary teams. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis, requiring submission of a statement of interest, CV, and design portfolio.

title: Research Assistant
department: Centre for Clinical Research and Biostatistics
description: This Research Assistant position supports the Centre for Clinical Research and Biostatistics in coordinating clinical research and data management projects with both local and international scope, particularly focused on pharmaceutical and medical device development. The role requires a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Science, strong organisational and project management skills, and proficiency in English, with preferred knowledge of statistics and programming. Key responsibilities include administrative project support, assistance with clinical research initiatives, involvement in protocol and case report form development, data management, and multi-centre study coordination. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral position in the Multimedia Communications Laboratory seeks a researcher with a PhD in a relevant discipline and demonstrated research experience to conduct independent research in mobile networking, Internet protocols, and machine learning. The role combines research responsibilities with co-supervision of graduate and undergraduate students. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract with the possibility of renewal based on mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position focuses on investigating gene regulation in skeletal muscle stem cells, muscle regeneration, and aging within an orthopaedic research group. Candidates must hold a PhD in Biology, Biomedical Science, Bioinformatics, or related fields obtained within the last five years, with a strong publication record and demonstrated ability to work independently and lead others. Responsibilities include conducting experiments and computational analyses, interpreting research findings, contributing to grant proposals and manuscripts, and mentoring junior group members. The role offers a one-year renewable contract.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s)
description: This position seeks postdoctoral fellows or research associates to conduct interdisciplinary research on brain structure and function under Dr. Sadia Shakil in a top-ranked biomedical engineering department. Candidates must hold a PhD in a relevant field (or a Master's degree plus four years of work experience) from a recognized institution, with strong foundations in mathematics, statistics, programming (MATLAB/Python), neuroimaging techniques (MRI/fMRI/EEG), and machine learning/AI. The role emphasizes analysis and model development combining multiple disciplines, and applicants should demonstrate strong English communication and technical writing abilities. Positions are available immediately on renewable one-year contracts.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: The position is for a Research Assistant supporting a large-scale AI education initiative aimed at integrating artificial intelligence into Hong Kong secondary school curricula. Core responsibilities include developing bilingual educational materials, creating hands-on courseware using programming languages and AI frameworks, and delivering training to schools and students. Applicants must hold a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, AI, Data Science or related fields, with strong English communication skills; desirable qualifications include AI expertise, Python proficiency, teaching experience, and Chinese language abilities. The role is offered initially on a two-year renewable contract basis.

title: Research Assistant
description: A Research Assistant is sought for clinical research in orthopaedics, requiring a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Biomedical Sciences, Biology, or related fields, along with proven research experience and fluency in English and Cantonese. The role involves managing clinical trials, handling tissue samples, obtaining research ethics approvals, coordinating projects, and preparing manuscripts, with prior experience in blood-taking and clinical data analysis being advantageous. The position is based at a hospital and focuses on sports medicine and osteoarthritis research, requiring independent work capability and collaborative team engagement. The appointment is initially a one-year contract, renewable based on performance.

title: Senior Research Assistant(s) (External Fund) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The School of Architecture seeks two research assistants to join a team studying Hong Kong's urban spaces using data-driven methods, focusing on public space activity patterns, public housing improvements, and elderly needs in dense urban areas. Applicants must hold a Master's degree in Architecture, Urban Design, Urban Analytics, or Computer Science with four years of relevant experience, along with strong proficiency in 3D modelling (Rhino3D, Grasshopper, Python) and written English. Senior positions involve leading Computer Vision analysis methods, managing data collection and processing, and conducting computational analysis in collaboration with the Principal Investigator, while Research Assistant roles focus on administrative support, digital modelling, and spatial analysis. The roles offer significant professional development opportunities including research skills training, co-authored publications, and exposure to international conferences and teaching, making them distinctive for individuals pursuing academic careers or PhD studies.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Centre for Cardiovascular Genomics and Medicine
description: This postdoctoral position involves conducting basic and clinical research in cardiovascular diseases with a focus on translational discoveries and precision medicine development within a dedicated genomics centre. The successful candidate will hold a PhD in biomedical science, bioinformatics, statistics, or related fields, with proficiency in both English and Cantonese, and ideally possess prior experience in genetics, genomics, statistics, or bioinformatics. The role requires strong teamwork and responsibility, and is initially offered on a one-year contract basis with potential renewal depending on performance. The position is distinctive for its emphasis on bridging research discoveries with clinical applications in cardiovascular medicine.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This full-time postdoctoral position within a translation research centre focuses on Chinese translation history, requiring a PhD in this field or a related discipline and a demonstrated track record of research publication. Primary responsibilities include producing articles for international publications, supporting the centre director's research initiatives, helping to organize academic events, and building collaborative networks within the discipline. Candidates must demonstrate capacity for multidisciplinary work and provide a resume, cover letter, academic credentials, and at least one reference letter. The initial one-year contract position is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement, with the start date flexible.

title: Research Fellow
description: This Research Fellow position in the Department of Electronic Engineering seeks a highly experienced researcher specializing in antenna engineering, microwave engineering, radar systems, or synthetic aperture radars, with a Ph.D. and at least eight years of postqualification experience in these fields. The successful candidate will conduct independent, top-tier research under professorial supervision, contribute to postgraduate student mentoring, and demonstrate excellence through publications and a strong academic record. Key requirements include fluency in English, organizational skills, self-motivation, and proven expertise preferably in antenna engineering or synthetic aperture radar systems. The role is offered initially on a one-year contract renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Joint CUHK-NTEC Clinical Research Management Office
description: This Research Assistant position supports clinical research operations at a hospital research centre, requiring a bachelor's degree in biological science or health-related fields and fluent English and Chinese communication skills. Key responsibilities include organizing clinical research projects, coordinating studies, collecting and managing clinical data, performing site monitoring and start-up activities, and providing general administrative support. The role particularly values strong interpersonal skills, responsibility, self-motivation, and MS Office proficiency, with clinical research experience preferred but not mandatory. The appointment is a one-year renewable contract position.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: Postdoctoral fellows are sought to join a pharmacy research team, developing natural language processing algorithms to extract content from Chinese and English articles. Candidates must hold a PhD in Pharmacy, Computer Science, or related fields from a reputable institution, with proficiency in at least one programming language and fluent English and Chinese communication skills. Key responsibilities include programming algorithm implementation, leading research projects, securing external funding, training team members, and preparing research documentation. The position offers a one-year renewable contract contingent on performance.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks postdoctoral fellows to support clinical and research programs across diverse ophthalmic specialties, including retinal diseases, glaucoma, ocular cancers, and genetic eye conditions. Candidates must hold a PhD in Biological Sciences (preferably ophthalmology or cancer research), demonstrate proficiency in molecular and cell biology techniques, and show strong independent and collaborative research abilities. Key responsibilities include coordinating research projects, analyzing data, preparing manuscripts and grant applications, and training junior staff. The position is offered on a one to two-year renewable contract, with preference given to applicants experienced in next-generation sequencing.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Centre for University and School Partnership
description: This Research Assistant position supports a university center focused on school partnerships, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Social Sciences or education with strong bilingual academic writing skills (English and Chinese) and knowledge of Hong Kong's educational context. The role involves assisting with research and evaluation of centre projects, compiling reports and deliverables, and providing project support, with opportunities for those with prior experience in development programme research or evaluation. Applicants must demonstrate self-motivation, organizational ability, and strong interpersonal skills, though less experienced candidates may be considered for a junior-level appointment. The position is initially offered on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: Candidates with a Bachelor's degree in Biology, Environmental Science, Marine Science, or related fields—or an Associate degree—are sought to support educational programmes and conservation initiatives centred on marine and coral ecosystems. Key responsibilities include delivering educational talks, organizing workshops and activities, liaising with stakeholders, managing budgets and programme materials, and maintaining social media presence, with occasional weekend and outdoor work required. Essential qualifications include bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English, strong communication and analytical skills, and demonstrated interest in marine conservation; scuba diving certification or prior educational experience is preferred. The role is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, with potential renewal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: Multiple postdoctoral positions are available to conduct research on smart materials and devices, with focus areas including fabrication, thermal management for energy efficiency, advanced sensing and camouflage technologies, flexible electronics, and soft grippers. Candidates must hold a PhD from a top 100 institution in STEM fields, with background in Materials Science/Engineering, Energy, Electronic Engineering, or related physical sciences, alongside strong analytical and communication skills, demonstrated academic excellence, and hands-on experience in materials and device fabrication. Appointments are initially for one year on a contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement. Applications should include a CV, transcripts, representative publications, and demonstration projects.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Sports Science and Physical Education
description: This full-time research assistant position requires a Bachelor's degree or higher in Physical Education, Sports Science, Exercise Science, Rehabilitation, Public Health, or related fields, along with fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese. The role involves recruiting school participants, collecting and analyzing research data, organizing and delivering physical activity interventions and workshops, and supporting other departmental tasks. Strong communication skills and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively are essential, with prior experience working with children and youth with special educational needs being advantageous. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Associate
department: Hong Kong Institute of Asia-Pacific Studies
description: This role involves supervising a team of telephone interviewers at a survey research laboratory with 60 workstations, overseeing data collection operations during evening shifts (2:30–10:30 pm for full-time or 6:30–10:30 pm for part-time positions). Candidates must hold a Master's degree, possess at least ten years of relevant post-qualification experience, demonstrate fluency in Cantonese and spoken English, and exhibit strong problem-solving and organisational abilities, with survey management experience preferred. The position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement, and supports the laboratory's mission of providing professional survey services to researchers and conducting public opinion polls.

title: Research Assistant
description: A Research Assistant position is available to support investigations into cardiomyocyte differentiation, heart development, and regeneration using cultured mammalian cells, zebrafish, and mouse models, alongside adeno-associated virus techniques.
