The successful candidate will require a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences or a related field, with foundational experience in molecular and cellular biology and biochemistry, alongside proficiency in standard laboratory equipment and techniques. Desirable qualifications include recent hands-on laboratory experience, molecular biology competency, CRISPR/Cas9 knowledge, and zebrafish handling skills. The role is offered on a one-year contract basis, commencing immediately, with renewal contingent upon performance and mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position focuses on investigating cardiomyocyte differentiation, heart development, and heart regeneration using cultured mammalian cells, zebrafish, and mouse models, with applications of adeno-associated viruses. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Biomedical Sciences or related fields and demonstrate strong laboratory experience in molecular and cellular biology, with desirable expertise in CRISPR/Cas9, mouse surgery, and zebrafish handling. Responsibilities include designing and executing research projects, presenting findings at conferences, writing manuscripts, and receiving mentorship in grant application writing. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Technical Officer (Digital Construction Recording Project) (S&T)
description: This position supports a digital documentation project that captures construction activities on campus through photography, video, and 360-degree imaging for educational purposes. The Technical Officer will coordinate with contractors and site teams to execute recording schedules and catalogue the resulting data to make it accessible for teaching. The role requires a degree or higher diploma in construction-related fields such as Civil Engineering, Building Studies, Construction Management, or Surveying, and is distinctive in bridging construction industry knowledge with academic support by transforming site records into classroom materials.

title: Project Manager (Digital Construction Recording Project) (S&T)
description: This position manages a digital documentation initiative for a campus construction project, transforming the build into an educational resource by recording critical construction phases and capturing metrics across multiple disciplines. The role requires collaboration with construction teams and academic staff to develop recording schedules while supervising technical equipment and infrastructure, and coordinating with external consultants and contractors to minimize disruption. Key requirements include project management expertise, technical proficiency, and ability to balance comprehensive documentation with operational constraints. The role is distinctive in bridging construction management and academic teaching through innovative digital recording technology.

title: Assistant Accounting Officer (General Ledger) (FO)
description: The position involves preparing journal entries and supporting financial schedules, assisting in developing standard operating procedures for general ledger workflows, and performing other assigned duties within the Finance Office. Candidates must have at least five HKDSE Level 2 subjects (including English, Chinese, and Mathematics) and preferably three years of accounting experience, with Oracle Finance system knowledge being advantageous. The role offers competitive remuneration including base salary, end-of-contract gratuity, discretionary incentive payments, and comprehensive benefits such as annual leave, professional development sponsorships, medical and dental coverage, and life insurance.

title: Development and Alumni Affairs Officer (DAAO)
description: This role supports fundraising and alumni engagement at an applied sciences university, involving donor cultivation activities, alumni communications management, and database administration. The ideal candidate requires a bachelor's degree, minimum two years of professional experience, and trilingual fluency in English, Cantonese, and Putonghua, along with strong organizational skills and digital marketing knowledge. The position emphasizes independent work, meticulous attention to detail, and the ability to juggle multiple projects, with occasional travel required. Distinctively, the role bridges the university's applied sciences focus with alumni and donor relations while supporting institutional growth into the Greater Bay Area.

title: Senior Manager (Human Resources) (HRO)
description: This Senior Manager position in the Human Resources Office oversees the development and implementation of HR strategies while leading a team delivering services across Schools and Offices, managing employee relations, complaints, and disciplinary matters. The role requires a bachelor's degree in Human Resources Management, Business Administration, or related field, alongside strong leadership, problem-solving, and analytical skills, with preference given to candidates with advanced qualifications. The position offers distinctive appeal through involvement in shaping HR functions within an institution experiencing significant growth and regional expansion into a major economic zone.

title: Student Affairs Manager (SAO)
description: This role manages the Student Affairs Office's First Year Experience Programme, serving as its primary architect to foster student engagement and smooth transitions into university life. The position requires driving continuous improvement across all SAO programmes through assessment frameworks, data-driven insights, and operational excellence while developing performance indicators to measure effectiveness. The manager will coordinate global learning opportunities by collaborating with academic departments and external partners to deliver international experiences. Additional responsibilities include supporting evidence-based practices among staff and performing duties assigned by the Director of Student Affairs.

title: Assistant Librarian I / II (Library Systems and Technology) (LIB)
description: This position manages the daily operations and strategic development of library technology infrastructure, including integrated library systems, discovery platforms, and related digital tools. The Assistant Librarian will develop technology initiatives, establish systems policies, and assess emerging technologies to improve service delivery, while also contributing to collection development and academic liaison activities. Candidates must hold a relevant qualification and demonstrate 2-6 years of post-qualification experience in academic library systems, with demonstrated technical expertise in integrated library management and digital platforms. The role combines technical proficiency with a service-oriented approach to support institutional teaching, research, and community engagement objectives.

title: Public Affairs Officer (Multimedia Design) (PAO)
description: This position requires designing and producing diverse multimedia content—including videos, graphics, motion graphics, and social media posts—to enhance the institution's digital marketing presence across platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, and Xiaohongshu. The role demands strong technical skills in video production, graphic design, motion graphics, and social media creation, alongside the ability to manage WordPress website maintenance while collaborating with internal teams to develop visually cohesive content aligned with brand strategy. The distinctive aspect lies in its emphasis on applied multimedia design that directly supports institutional branding and community engagement as the organization expands into the Greater Bay Area, requiring awareness of emerging design trends and flexibility in a rapidly evolving digital landscape.

title: Research Associate (Digital Health) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This Research Associate position supports digital health initiatives within the nursing and health sciences school, specializing in wearable devices, mobile applications, and health sensors designed to enable personalized patient care. Responsibilities span project management, development of AI-based predictive tools and applications, construction of hardware and software platforms, team coordination, and comprehensive data analysis including database design. The role requires a PhD in Electronic Engineering, Computer Science, Medical Engineering, or related field, with demonstrated expertise in backend development frameworks (PHP, Node.js, or Python) and frontend technologies. What distinguishes this position is its unique intersection of advanced digital technologies and artificial intelligence applied directly to healthcare challenges, bridging engineering expertise with practical clinical outcomes.

title: Assistant Finance Manager (Procurement) (FO)
description: This Assistant Finance Manager (Procurement) role supports the Finance Manager by leading procurement operations, managing inventory and fixed assets, and developing sourcing strategies to support organizational objectives. The position requires a degree in purchasing, supply management, or business administration plus at least three years of post-qualification experience in procurement, tendering, and vendor management, ideally from larger organizations. Essential competencies include strong planning and problem-solving skills, fluency in English and Chinese, and proficiency with Microsoft Office and procurement management systems. The role combines strategic procurement development with day-to-day operational management, including oversight of corporate insurance programs.

title: Research Assistant II (R6586) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This Research Assistant II position supports an exergaming research project focused on healthy aging, requiring a Bachelor's degree or higher in Psychology, Education Psychology, or a health-related field. Primary responsibilities include assisting with data collection and analysis, managing databases, preparing study materials, and instructing older adult participants while monitoring their engagement and well-being. Preferred qualifications include experience with fNIRS data collection and analysis, strong communication and problem-solving abilities, and prior research experience with older populations. The role offers competitive compensation including salary, gratuity, generous leave entitlements, professional development support, and comprehensive benefits.

title: Assistant Finance Manager (Payment) (FO)
description: This position involves supervising a payment operations team and overseeing accounts payable functions, including payment scheduling, reconciliations, and month-end closing processes. The successful candidate must possess a degree in Accountancy or a related field with at least 3 years of post-qualification experience in larger organizations, with professional accounting qualifications such as CPA strongly preferred. Key requirements include strong analytical and organizational skills, meticulous attention to detail, ability to manage competing deadlines under pressure, and fluency in English and Chinese. The role is distinctive for prioritizing process improvement and system automation to enhance departmental efficiency.

title: Research Assistant I/II (Project Ref. No.: CS/106/2412/RA) (S&T)
description: The position supports a research project developing a driving performance analysis system for driving tests, with primary responsibilities including frontend user interface implementation using modern frameworks and backend server logic/API development in collaboration with the engineering team. Candidates must hold a Master's degree or above in a computing-related discipline (Electronic Engineering, Computer Science, AI, Software Engineering, etc.) for Research Assistant I, or a Bachelor's degree for Research Assistant II in the same fields. Full-stack developer experience and a valid driving licence are advantageous but not required. The role offers competitive remuneration including base salary and end-of-contract gratuity.

title: Assistant Finance Manager (Financial Accounting) (FO)
description: This role requires a qualified accountant with a minimum of 4 years' post-qualification experience to manage the Financial Accounting team and oversee general accounting operations. Primary responsibilities include preparing consolidated financial statements and management accounts, managing externally-funded project reporting, and driving process improvements to enhance efficiency. The successful candidate must be self-motivated, well-organized, and capable of performing effectively under pressure, with strong communication and service-oriented capabilities. The position offers competitive compensation, professional development support, and comprehensive benefits including medical, dental, and life insurance coverage.

title: Assistant Industry Partnerships Manager (SAO)
description: This role manages and develops industry partnerships for a Student Affairs Office, coordinating with internal departments and external stakeholders in corporate, government, and non-profit sectors to create opportunities in career placements, entrepreneurship, and curriculum development. The position requires a bachelor's degree and at least 6 years of experience building partnerships specifically in Hong Kong and mainland China, with demonstrated expertise in post-secondary industry collaborations and student development initiatives. Key responsibilities include conducting industry research to guide partnership strategy and producing marketing materials to showcase institutional capabilities. The role is distinctive for its emphasis on bridging academic institutions with diverse external organizations to enhance student outcomes and ensure educational offerings align with market demands within an applied sciences context.

title: Assistant Academic Support Officer (S&T)
description: This Assistant Academic Support Officer position provides operational support to the School of Science and Technology across academic programmes, quality assurance, research, and human resources functions while managing stakeholder relations. Applicants require at least 5 HKDSE Level 2 subjects (including English, Chinese, and Mathematics) or equivalent qualifications, preferably with prior administrative experience, and must demonstrate fluent bilingual communication skills in English and Chinese. The role demands self-motivation, independent working ability, quick learning capacity, and strong responsibility. The position offers competitive remuneration including salary, end-of-contract gratuity, annual leave, professional development support, and comprehensive health and insurance coverage.

title: Assistant Human Resources Officer (HRO)
description: This position involves supporting a range of HR functions including compensation, benefits, performance management, talent development, and workforce planning, while maintaining HR records. The ideal candidate holds a degree in Human Resources Management or a related field and meets minimum education requirements including HKDSE Level 2 qualifications in English, Chinese, and Mathematics. Strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, preferably with Putonghua, is essential for the role. The package includes competitive salary, gratuity, performance incentives, generous leave, professional development support, and comprehensive medical, dental, and life insurance benefits.

title: Lecturer (Health Care) (LiPACE)
department: Li Ka Shing School of Professional and Continuing Education
description: This Lecturer position focuses on teaching foundational medical science, particularly pharmacology and medicines, to vocational and professional education students across full-time and part-time programmes. The role requires healthcare expertise combined with pedagogical skill in engaging diverse, non-traditional learners of varying ages and backgrounds. The position is distinctive for its emphasis on applied sciences and professional training within an institution specializing in vocational education, with potential involvement in regional initiatives. The successful candidate will contribute to the institution's mission of providing accessible, quality education to the broader Hong Kong community.

title: Lecturer (Health and Molecular Testing) (LiPACE)
department: Li Ka Shing School of Professional and Continuing Education
description: This position is for a Lecturer in Health and Molecular Testing within the professional and continuing education division of an applied sciences university. The role involves teaching and curriculum development in health and molecular testing across both full-time and part-time professional education programmes. Key requirements would include expertise in health and molecular testing, teaching experience in vocational or professional education, and the ability to contribute to programme development aligned with industry standards. The distinctive aspect is the focus on applied, practice-oriented education serving diverse adult learners through flexible study modes within an institutional context committed to vocational excellence and community engagement.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer (Digital Business) (LiPACE)
department: Li Ka Shing School of Professional and Continuing Education
description: This position is a Senior Lecturer or Lecturer role in Digital Business within a professional and continuing education institution specializing in vocational training and lifelong learning. The post holder will design and deliver digital business curriculum to diverse, non-traditional learners including mature students and working professionals, while conducting applied research aligned with institutional priorities. Key requirements include digital business expertise, strong teaching skills adapted to adult learners, and the ability to connect academic concepts with practical industry applications. The role is distinctive for its focus on professional development and career advancement rather than traditional academic education, serving students seeking skills enhancement and career progression outside conventional university pathways.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer (Business Administration and Management) (LiPACE)
department: Li Ka Shing School of Professional and Continuing Education
description: A professional education institution seeks a Senior Lecturer or Lecturer in Business Administration and Management to join its School of Professional and Continuing Education. The role is distinctive for supporting vocational and professional education delivery across full-time post-secondary and part-time professional programs, alongside corporate training initiatives. The position will contribute to the school's mission of providing accessible, quality education to diverse learners of varying ages, backgrounds, and career stages. The ideal candidate will align with the institution's strategic focus on applied skills, innovative teaching, and preparing talent to meet Hong Kong's evolving industry needs.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor (Nursing) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This nursing faculty position requires leading course development and delivery while conducting research and contributing to institutional service at a student-centred university. Candidates must demonstrate teaching excellence, innovation in curriculum design, a strong publication record, and success in securing external research funding. The role emphasizes applied professional education aligned with industry needs and focuses on strategic research areas including gerontechnology and personalised care. The appointment level and compensation will be determined based on qualifications and experience.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor (Mental Health) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: The position seeks an Associate or Assistant Professor in Mental Health for the School of Nursing and Health Sciences to undertake course planning, teaching delivery, research leadership, and institutional service. Candidates must hold a PhD in Mental Health or a related discipline, demonstrate academic leadership and strong teaching capabilities, possess a solid publication record in peer-reviewed venues, and show evidence of securing external research funding. The university particularly values innovation in curriculum design, postgraduate student supervision, and contributions to the academic profession and broader community. Appointment level will depend on the candidate's experience and achievements in these areas.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer (Mental Health) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This role offers a Senior Lecturer or Lecturer position in Mental Health Nursing, requiring candidates to lead course coordination, deliver classroom teaching, conduct clinical assessments, mentor students, and pursue scholarly research. The successful applicant must hold professional registration as a mental health nurse with a minimum of three years' clinical experience and a demonstrated research track record. Appointment level and salary are commensurate with academic qualifications, leadership experience, and publication record. The position includes competitive benefits such as professional development support, generous leave entitlements, medical and dental coverage, and potential housing assistance for eligible overseas candidates under 50.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer (Clinical) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This position requires a registered nursing professional to teach, coordinate courses, conduct clinical assessments, and mentor students in nursing and health sciences. Applicants must have at least five years of professional nursing experience, ideally in adult, child, mental health, or related specialties. Beyond teaching, the role demands involvement in research, scholarly activities, and institutional service. The specific appointment level and salary are determined by the candidate's qualifications, teaching experience, research productivity, and academic leadership capabilities.

title: Part-time Senior Lecturer/Lecturer (Physiotherapy) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This part-time position seeks a Senior Lecturer or Lecturer in Physiotherapy to assist with course planning, development, coordination, and delivery across the institution's physiotherapy programmes at multiple academic levels. Candidates must hold a Master's degree or higher in Physiotherapy or a related field, with at least five years of post-qualification professional experience, proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, and preferably prior higher education teaching experience. The role involves fewer than 68 hours of work per four-week period on a temporary contract through June 2026, with statutory benefits provided.

title: Course Developer (Freelance) (LANG 3331AED and LANG 3332AED) (A&SS)
department: School of Arts and Social Sciences
description: This freelance position involves developing two sociolinguistics courses (LANG 3331AED and LANG 3332AED) for a School of Arts and Social Sciences, with primary responsibilities including writing course units aligned with learning outcomes, creating self-tests and activities, and designing assessments that allow students to apply learned skills and concepts. The ideal candidate holds a higher degree in Linguistics with specialization in Sociolinguistics or a closely related field, and should demonstrate excellent organizational skills, meticulous attention to detail, and ability to work under pressure. Teaching, research, and course design experience are advantageous. The role is offered on a freelance basis.

title: Part-time Lecturer of EDU 3355ECF (2026 Summer Term) (E&L)
description: This part-time lecturer position involves teaching EDU 3355ECF during the 2026 summer term within the School of Education and Languages, serving undergraduate degree programmes in Chinese language teaching, applied Chinese language, and related fields. Candidates must have a minimum of five years of experience in student discipline, guidance, and pastoral care roles within local secondary or primary schools. Teaching experience or teacher training qualifications are preferred. The role is suited for experienced education professionals seeking part-time work aligned with the university's commitment to applied professional education and community-focused teaching.

title: Part-time Research Assistants I /II (Project No. R7043) (A&SS)
department: School of Arts and Social Sciences
description: This part-time position involves assisting researchers on a longitudinal study examining how poor housing conditions affect child development in Hong Kong's subdivided units, using mixed-method analysis. Key responsibilities include coordinating with academic staff and NGO partners, conducting quantitative and qualitative research, and supporting project operations, with occasional weekend and evening work required. Candidates must have research experience, proficiency in both English and Cantonese, strong computer skills including data analysis software (NVivo), and the ability to work independently under deadline pressure. The role is contracted for 16.5 hours per week from March to June 2026.

title: Part-time Lecturer (PG) (2026 Summer) (A&SS Creative Arts)
department: School of Arts and Social Sciences
description: This part-time position involves teaching and curriculum development for postgraduate creative arts programmes during summer 2026 (May-August), including lecturing, tutoring, grading assessments, and designing complementary course topics. Applicants must hold a higher degree in Creative Writing, Chinese Language and Literature, or related field, with tertiary-level teaching experience and professional expertise; PhD holders are preferred. Candidates must be fluent in both Cantonese and Putonghua and should be willing to work daytime and/or evening shifts on campus, with the contract typically requiring under 68 hours per four-week period. The role emphasizes the institution's commitment to applied sciences education and innovative teaching within a student-centred university framework.

title: Part-time Research Assistant II (R6887) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This part-time position supports laboratory-based research in the School of Nursing and Health Sciences, involving conducting experiments with DNA/RNA sequencing techniques, preparing research reports under supervision, and assisting with related research activities as directed by the Principal Investigator. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Biological, Biomedical, or Biotechnology-related sciences with demonstrated practical experience in molecular biology techniques such as DNA/RNA extraction, cell culture, PCR, gel electrophoresis, and Western blotting. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, self-motivated, reliable with deadlines, and capable of independent work. The position is offered as a temporary part-time contract with statutory benefits.

title: Temporary Research Assistant II (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This temporary full-time research assistant position (3-6 months) in health economics supports project coordination, survey design, participant recruitment, and data management for both qualitative and quantitative research. The role requires a bachelor's degree in social sciences or related fields, fluency in written Traditional Chinese and spoken Cantonese, and proficiency with Canvas and Qualtrics platforms, with R and STATA knowledge preferred. Candidates must demonstrate strong organizational skills, independence, and flexibility to work extended hours. The position is distinctive for combining health economics research methodology with community engagement and multilingual capabilities.

title: Temporary Laboratory Officer (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This temporary full-time position supports nursing and health sciences teaching by preparing laboratory materials, demonstrating experiments, and providing instrument training to students. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in a biomedical discipline with at least two years of laboratory teaching support experience, with preference given to those with MLT registration and specialized credentials in areas such as Clinical Chemistry or Medical Microbiology. The role requires strong attention to detail, independence, and the ability to manage deadlines while maintaining laboratory safety and security standards. The position offers statutory benefits and represents a practical opportunity for laboratory professionals to contribute directly to health sciences education.

title: Course Developer (Master of Science in Diagnostic Ultrasonography) (Freelance) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This freelance position involves developing course materials and examination questions for a new Master of Science in Diagnostic Ultrasonography programme offered in English. The role requires a higher degree in Health Sciences, radiology, or medical imaging, with preferred experience in distance learning or part-time course instruction and prior involvement in course material development for higher education institutions. The programme focuses on comprehensive ultrasonography training, emphasizing abdominal and vascular imaging while introducing broader imaging modalities, and the course developer will support the School of Nursing and Health Sciences in creating educational content that builds students' technical proficiency and clinical knowledge.

title: Temporary Clerk (REG)
description: This temporary full-time clerical position in the Registry department provides frontline student services across multiple communication channels—counter, email, and telephone—while supporting admissions and enrolment operations. Applicants must hold Hong Kong secondary education qualifications (HKDSE Level 2 or equivalent), demonstrate bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese with preferred Putonghua skills, and possess competent Microsoft Office capabilities. The role offers staff development opportunities within an institution focused on applied sciences and professional education. Employment includes statutory benefits on a temporary full-time basis.

title: Temporary Assistant Officer (HR team) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This is a temporary full-time assistant officer position within the human resources team of the School of Nursing and Health Sciences. Candidates should hold a recognized degree (preferably in human resources management or related field) and demonstrate strong proficiency in English, Chinese, and mathematics at HKDSE Level 2 or equivalent, along with excellent computer, organizational, and interpersonal skills in both English and Chinese languages. The role supports HR functions within a modern university focused on professional education and strategic research across areas including gerontechnology, personalized care, and smart city initiatives. Employment will be offered on a temporary contract with statutory benefits provided.

title: Part-time Research Associate / Assistant I/II / Technical Assistant (Project No: R2091) (B&A)
department: Lee Shau Kee School of Business and Administration
description: This is a part-time research position within the School of Business and Administration supporting a project investigating gender inequality among esports practitioners. The role involves participant recruitment, data collection, interview transcription, qualitative data coding using NVivo, and report preparation under the Principal Investigator's direction. Candidates require a Bachelor's degree in Sport Management, Sport Marketing, Sport Psychology, or related social science discipline, with demonstrated experience in qualitative research methods, proficiency in English and Chinese (with Mandarin transcription as an advantage), and strong attention to detail and self-motivation.

title: Temporary Research Assistant II (R6943) (S&T)
description: This temporary full-time research assistant position supports an environmental chemistry project examining per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances in urban stormwater using chemical profiling and bioactivity analysis. Responsibilities include laboratory and technical support, environmental sampling, sample preparation, report writing, and other research activities under supervisor direction. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree in Chemistry, Analytical Chemistry, Environmental Science, or a related field, with preferred experience in laboratory analytical work and instrumental techniques such as LC-MS/MS and GC-MS. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, self-motivated, and capable of working independently to meet deadlines while working on a temporary contract with statutory benefits.

title: Temporary Facilities Officer (Interior Design) (11-month contract) (FMO)
description: This is an 11-month temporary full-time position in the Facilities Management Office responsible for interior design-related facilities work. Candidates must have at least five HKDSE subjects at Level 2 or above (including English, Chinese, and Mathematics) and preferably a recognized degree, along with strong bilingual communication skills in English and Chinese. The role offers statutory benefits and requires submission through the university's eRecruitment System, with applicants notified of outcomes within eight weeks of the closing date.

title: Part-Time Research Assistant I (Psychology of Sports, Exercise, and Health) (A&SS)
department: School of Arts and Social Sciences
description: This is a part-time research assistant position supporting faculty-led research in sports psychology, physical activity, and health education. The role involves coordinating mental health workshops at schools and community settings, preparing experimental materials, liaising with schools and NGOs, and assisting with data collection on flexible schedules including evenings and weekends. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in psychology, social sciences, sports sciences, or health-related fields, with demonstrated experience in quantitative and qualitative research methods, proficiency in Microsoft Office and data analysis software, and strong bilingual communication skills in English and Cantonese.

title: Temporary Research Assistant I (R6671) (S&T)
description: This temporary full-time position assists a research team investigating sentiment analysis to distinguish between ChatGPT-generated and human-generated text within the School of Science and Technology. The role primarily involves preparing research papers and reports, supporting the Principal Investigator, and performing related research duties. Candidates must hold a Master's degree or higher in Engineering, Computer Science, Education Technology, or related fields, with demonstrated research experience in machine learning, artificial intelligence, data labeling, natural language processing, and computer vision. Proficiency in English communication and writing is preferred.

title: Part-time Lecturer (AI and healthcare technologies) [2026 Summer Term] (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This part-time summer teaching position in the School of Nursing and Health Sciences requires delivering courses on artificial intelligence and healthcare technologies, including lectures, tutorials, course development, and student assessment, with evening classes and a maximum 68-hour workload per four-week cycle. The ideal candidate holds a doctoral degree in computer science, health informatics, artificial intelligence, data science, or a related field, with proven tertiary-level teaching experience and expertise in big data analytics. The role emphasises practical skill development in smart healthcare technologies and supports institutional strategic priorities around gerontechnology and personalised care within an applied sciences context.

title: Part-time Lecturer for EDU 2201ECF (2026 Summer Term)
description: This is a part-time lecturer position for a summer term course (EDU 2201ECF) within the School of Education and Languages, teaching in either the Bachelor of Education (Putonghua and Chinese Education) or Bachelor of Arts (Chinese Linguistics) degree programmes. The role requires delivering course content to full-time students during the 2026 summer term on a part-time basis. Candidates should have relevant expertise in education, Chinese language teaching, or linguistics depending on the specific course assignment. The position is temporary and limited to the summer term duration, with applications considered unsuccessful if candidates are not contacted within eight weeks of the closing date.

title: Temporary / Part-time Research Assistant / Technical Assistant (Project No: R6839) (E&L)
description: This temporary part-time research position supports a Principal Investigator on a project investigating ethical AI applications in educational settings, requiring assistance with research instrument design, report preparation, and project tasks. The role requires either a Bachelor's degree or higher in Education, Educational Technology, or Social Sciences, or alternatively technical qualifications with specified HKDSE subjects, alongside fluency in written and spoken English and Cantonese. Preference is given to candidates with prior educational research experience and familiarity with AI in education. The position offers flexible contract arrangements (full-time or part-time) with potential renewal and standard institutional benefits.

title: Part-time Technical Assistant(s) (R6694) (S&T)
description: This is a part-time technical assistant position within the School of Science and Technology, primarily supporting a research project on antibiotic detection and degradation in wastewater. Responsibilities include assisting with report writing and conducting research activities under the direction of the Principal Investigator and supervisors. Candidates should hold at least five HKDSE Level 2 subjects (including English, Chinese, and Mathematics) or equivalent, with preference given to current science students, particularly those with Environmental Science or Chemistry backgrounds. The role requires strong organizational and interpersonal skills, and successful applicants will be appointed on a temporary part-time contract with statutory benefits.

title: Part-time Lecturer for PTH 3303ECF (2026 Summer Term) (E&L)
description: This is a part-time lecturer position for the summer term 2026, based in the School of Education and Languages, teaching PTH 3303ECF to students pursuing honours degrees in Putonghua and Chinese Teaching or Chinese Language Linguistics Research. The role requires expertise in education and language instruction, with the ability to deliver high-quality professional education aligned with industry standards. The position is temporary, spanning only the summer term, and candidates should be prepared for a decision timeline of up to eight weeks following the application deadline.

title: Full-time/Temporary Research Assistant I (Project No.: R7504) (E&L)
description: This is a full-time, temporary one-year research assistant position within the School of Education and Languages supporting a project on detecting driver intoxication through linguistic analysis. The role requires a Master's degree in computing, artificial intelligence, or related fields, with expert-level Python proficiency and experience in machine learning, language modeling, and database development. Key responsibilities include assisting with data collection and analysis, building language models, implementing deep learning and large language model technologies, and supporting the digitalization of research databases. Prior experience in language database compilation or language model development is advantageous, and the contract is renewable based on performance.

title: Associate Professor(s)/Assistant Professor(s)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: The Department of Accountancy is recruiting Assistant or Associate Professors with strong research credentials and a balanced commitment to research, teaching, and professional engagement in accounting. Applicants must hold a PhD and preferably professional accounting certification, with priority given to candidates specializing in corporate governance and sustainability, auditing and assurance, or related accounting areas. The position offers an opportunity to contribute to a liberal-arts-oriented institution with AACSB accreditation that emphasizes undergraduate education, research impact, and small-class teaching. Successful candidates will join a growing business school ranked among the top institutions in the Asia-Pacific region for employability and research quality.

title: Associate Professor/Assistant Professor (Full-time)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: The position is an Associate/Assistant Professor role in the Department of Economics and Finance, responsible for teaching specialist modules across finance, economics, real estate development, investment, and emerging areas like FinTech and ESG, while contributing to programme development and research activities. Candidates must hold a PhD in Finance, Economics, or related fields, with demonstrated teaching experience at tertiary level and a strong publication record, preferably with international academic exposure. For the real estate development specialization, preference is given to RICS chartered members with PhD qualifications. The role requires fluency in English and spoken Putonghua is advantageous, reflecting the institution's Hong Kong location and regional academic environment.

title: Lecturer
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This Lecturer position in Economics and Finance involves planning and delivering undergraduate and postgraduate modules across areas including finance, economics, real estate, investment, and emerging fields like FinTech and ESG, alongside contributions to programme development and student support. Candidates must hold a PhD in Finance, Economics, or a related discipline with solid teaching experience at tertiary level and a demonstrable research publication record, with international exposure preferred. The role combines teaching responsibilities with active research and scholarly engagement, reflecting the institution's commitment to both pedagogical excellence and impactful research output. This is a full-time appointment at a regionally-ranked liberal arts-oriented university emphasizing close student-teacher relationships and innovative degree programmes.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor (Full-time)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: The Department of Management is seeking full-time Associate Professor or Assistant Professor positions requiring a PhD degree and strong research credentials. Primary responsibilities include teaching across undergraduate and postgraduate programmes, conducting impactful research, supervising students, and contributing to departmental and university service. Ideal candidates will demonstrate excellence in their field, experience in management education, and a commitment to the institution's "Liberal + Professional" educational model that emphasizes critical thinking and social responsibility alongside professional development.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor (Full-time)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: The Department of Management seeks a full-time Associate Professor or Assistant Professor with a PhD and strong publication record in macro-level management fields such as International Business, Strategic Management, Entrepreneurship, or Sustainability. The successful candidate will be responsible for research, teaching at tertiary level, and student engagement within a liberal arts-oriented institution that emphasizes close faculty-student relationships and innovative pedagogy. Key requirements include demonstrated excellence in top-tier journal publications, tertiary teaching experience, and commitment to scholarly activities. The position offers a competitive salary commensurate with qualifications, with an anticipated start date of January 2026.

title: Assistant Professor (Full-time)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This full-time Assistant Professor position in Marketing seeks a PhD-qualified scholar with a strong publication record in top-tier journals and demonstrated teaching experience at the tertiary level. The role requires candidates to teach across diverse marketing modules, conduct research with publication aspirations in world-leading journals, and actively engage in service to students and stakeholders. Applicants must show strong commitment to both scholarly activities and student engagement, with the position offering a start date of January or August 2026 and salary based on qualifications and experience.

title: Assistant Professor
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
department: Department of Mathematics, Statistics and Insurance
description: This Assistant Professor position in the Department of Mathematics, Statistics and Insurance seeks a doctorate holder with strong research credentials and publications in statistics, applied mathematics, actuarial science, insurance, or related fields. Primary responsibilities include teaching courses in data science, actuarial science, insurance, and risk management; conducting research and publishing in leading peer-reviewed journals; and facilitating industry connections through professional talks and events for students. The role also encompasses scholarly contributions and administrative duties within a liberal arts-oriented university that emphasizes undergraduate education, small-class teaching, and research impact across the Asia-Pacific region.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This position involves teaching and curriculum development across undergraduate, postgraduate, and general education levels in social science, along with research and administrative duties. Candidates must hold a doctorate in Political Science, Sociology, or East Asian Studies with demonstrated expertise in social science research methods, substantial tertiary teaching experience, and administrative background in higher education. The role requires ability to teach global affairs and social sustainability with particular focus on Asian contexts, addressing topics such as urbanisation, international relations, and societal transformation. The institution is a regionally-ranked liberal arts university emphasizing small-class teaching, close student-faculty relationships, and research impact.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor (Full-time)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
department: Department of Supply Chain and Information Management
description: This full-time position in the Department of Supply Chain and Information Management seeks an Associate or Assistant Professor to teach specialist modules, contribute to programme development, conduct research, and fulfill administrative duties. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in a related field with strong expertise in areas such as Management Science, Supply Chain Management, Transportation, or Aviation, along with proven teaching and research experience at tertiary level and a solid publication record. Preference is given to applicants with industry experience and proficiency in both English and Chinese. The role emphasizes the institution's commitment to research-informed teaching within a liberal arts framework focused on student-centered education and community engagement.

title: Part-time Instructor(s) (Action Learning and Experiential Teaching)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: The position is a part-time instructor role within the School of Business focused on action learning and experiential teaching methodologies. The instructor will be responsible for designing and delivering interactive, experience-based courses that develop students' practical skills and critical thinking within the liberal arts-professional education model. Ideal candidates should possess expertise in experiential pedagogy and demonstrate ability to facilitate active learning environments that prepare students for professional practice. This role is distinctive given the School's recent AACSB accreditation, pursuit of EQUIS accreditation, and commitment to innovative teaching approaches that distinguish it as a leading business school in Asia.

title: Assistant Professor
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This Assistant Professor position in the School of Transdisciplinary Studies focuses on developing and delivering interdisciplinary academic programmes, including designing curricula, preparing accreditation documents, and teaching courses aligned with the school's mission. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in fields such as health studies, social policy, higher education leadership, or related disciplines, with substantial higher education teaching experience and demonstrated expertise in interdisciplinary programme development. The role requires an active research and publication record, strong collaborative and leadership capabilities, and a commitment to student engagement and scholarly activities. The position supports the university's distinctive "Liberal + Professional" educational model and its goal of establishing itself as a leading private institution in the region.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
department: School of Translation and Foreign Languages
description: This position seeks an Associate or Assistant Professor for the School of Translation and Foreign Languages to teach and coordinate undergraduate and postgraduate programmes, develop course materials and assessments, provide academic advising, and supervise student projects. Applicants must hold a doctoral degree in translation studies or a related field, with demonstrated experience in tertiary-level teaching and a strong research record. The role requires active participation in scholarly research and publication, committee work, and programme quality assurance, along with contributions to institutional governance and student engagement activities. The position offers the opportunity to work within a well-ranked liberal-arts-oriented institution recognised for its undergraduate education quality, innovative curricula, and regional academic standing.

title: Manager (Facility Management Services)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This position manages facility management services for a private liberal arts university with over 7,000 students across six schools. The role is responsible for overseeing campus facilities operations within the Campus Development and Management Office, supporting the institution's commitment to maintaining award-winning green campus infrastructure. The ideal candidate will have expertise in facility management and the ability to contribute to the university's operational excellence while supporting its strategic priorities in education, research, and student development. This is a distinctive opportunity at a regionally-recognized institution that emphasizes integrated campus living and learning environments alongside academic achievement.

title: Director of School of Transdisciplinary Studies
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: The Director of the School of Transdisciplinary Studies will lead one of six schools at a Hong Kong-based liberal arts university serving over 7,000 students. The role requires overseeing academic programme development, faculty management, and student support within a transdisciplinary framework that emphasises critical thinking, innovation, and social responsibility. The successful candidate should demonstrate strong leadership in advancing research impact, fostering collaborative teaching approaches, and aligning the school's offerings with the institution's "Liberal + Professional" educational model and its established reputation for excellence in employability and community engagement.

title: Procurement Assistant
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This Procurement Assistant role within the Finance Office supports the university's procurement operations through daily administrative tasks including sourcing, quotation management, document preparation, purchase order compilation, and supplier database maintenance. The position also handles outsourced catering service administration, general insurance matters, and routine document management including filing and scanning. Candidates must hold a higher diploma or associate degree in Business Administration or related field, demonstrate proficiency in Windows and Microsoft Office applications, and possess strong English and Chinese language skills alongside excellent communication, organizational, and problem-solving abilities. The role requires attention to detail, multitasking capability, and the ability to work collaboratively as part of a team.

title: Executive Officer
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This Executive Officer position in the Department of Computer Science involves performing administrative duties, managing student enquiries, and serving as secretary to departmental committees and working groups. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree with preferably three years of relevant experience, strong proficiency in Microsoft Office and Chinese word processing, and fluent English and Chinese (with Putonghua as an advantage). Key qualities sought include excellent interpersonal and communication skills, self-motivation, reliability, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively, with prior experience in higher education being advantageous. The role offers competitive salary based on qualifications and experience, with two vacancies available.

title: Assistant Officer
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This position is an administrative role within the Campus Development and Management Office responsible for providing clerical and operational support to the facility management team, including helpdesk services, venue bookings, vehicle services, event coordination, and mail distribution. Key responsibilities include handling facility-related inquiries, liaising between internal and external parties, supervising contractors and service providers, managing venue setup, and compiling FM statistics and reports. Applicants must hold a higher diploma or associate degree or above and have at least three years of relevant working experience, with facility or property management experience being advantageous. The role requires strong organizational and communication skills to support the day-to-day operations of campus facilities and services.

title: Assistant Librarian II (Collections and Technical Services)
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This Assistant Librarian II position manages collection development and technical services for an academic library, encompassing material assessment, selection and deselection across all formats, faculty liaison for curriculum support, and policy development. The role requires a Master's in Library Science or equivalent qualification with a minimum of two years of post-qualification experience in a supervisory capacity, alongside expertise in collection assessment, bibliographic standards (AACR2, RDA, LCSH, MARC21), and academic faculty engagement. The distinctive aspects include responsibility for evaluating donated materials, generating collection analytics, and demonstrating knowledge of artificial intelligence applications in library contexts. The position also includes reference desk duties and contributes to institutional strategic planning.

title: Security Superintendent
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This Security Superintendent role within the Campus Development and Management Office requires managing daily security operations across campus, including staff scheduling, access control systems, CCTV monitoring, and emergency preparedness coordination. The position demands candidates with an accredited higher diploma or degree in Security Management or related field, a QAS certificate, and security personnel permit, alongside minimum 8 years of security operations experience with at least 5 years in a supervisory capacity. Key responsibilities include developing and enforcing security policies, conducting risk assessments, leading incident investigations, liaising with law enforcement, and managing security budgets. The role is distinctive in its emphasis on comprehensive campus security oversight at a growing private liberal-arts university with over 7,000 students and multiple facilities requiring integrated security management.

title: Executive Officer
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This Executive Officer role in the Registry department involves coordinating admission and recruitment activities with Schools, Departments, and external stakeholders, including organizing promotional events for prospective students and managing recruitment data and statistics. Key responsibilities include preparing reports, serving as secretary to committees, and supporting general Registry functions. The ideal candidate should hold a bachelor's degree with at least 3 years of relevant experience in student recruitment or event organization, demonstrate proficiency in English and Chinese (both Cantonese and Putonghua), and possess strong computer literacy including Chinese word processing. The position is distinctive for its focus on bridging institutional communication and student recruitment within a growing private liberal-arts university context.

title: 中文系
university_full: Hang Seng University of Hong Kong
description: This part-time research assistant position supports a Research Grants Council-funded project on the reception and reinterpretation of Qu Yuan and the *Chu ci* during the Republican period. The role involves collecting and organizing research materials, writing summaries and research compendiums, and preparing academic papers and publications. Applicants must hold a master's degree in Chinese or a related field and demonstrate strong capabilities in handling and writing academic literature in both Chinese and English, along with careful attention to detail and proactive work habits. Prior familiarity with the relevant research area is preferred.

title: Associate Vice-President (Quality Assurance)
description: Associate Vice-President (Quality Assurance). Please visit the application link for full details.

title: Director
description: The position requires a director to lead the Information Technology Services Centre with extensive managerial experience in higher education administration, ideally gained from a similar role in local or international institutions. Candidates must hold a postgraduate qualification, with a doctorate preferred, and demonstrate at least five years of relevant senior-level administrative experience. Fluency in both English and Chinese, in written and spoken forms, is essential. This role is distinctive in requiring both advanced academic credentials and proven leadership experience specifically within higher education IT service environments.

title: Clinic Manager cum Senior Lecturer
department: S.K. Yee School of Health Sciences
description: This combined clinical and academic role involves establishing and expanding a physiotherapy service at a practice clinic while delivering patient care and supervising undergraduate physiotherapy students. The position requires a Master's degree in physiotherapy or related field, current registration and practicing certificate as a physiotherapist in Hong Kong, and at least 5 years of professional experience at senior level. Key responsibilities include clinical service delivery, clinical education, research facilitation, and institutional development. The role is distinctive in its dual emphasis on clinical practice expansion and academic teaching within an integrated education centre.

title: [Full-time / Part-time] Associate Professors / Assistant Professors / Senior Lecturers
department: School of Business and Hospitality Management
description: The position requires teaching across multiple subject areas at sub-degree through postgraduate levels, alongside scholarly research and administrative duties in business and hospitality management. Candidates for Assistant Professor must hold a PhD in a relevant discipline plus relevant teaching or professional experience, with preference for those demonstrating strong undergraduate/postgraduate teaching records, programme management expertise, and proven research output. Senior Lecturer applicants require a Master's degree and professional membership, along with a minimum of three years of supervisory or managerial industry experience. Strong English proficiency is essential, with Chinese language skills preferred.

title: Research Professor
description: This role requires leading the research and scholarly activities of a school while producing high-impact international research and fostering a strong research culture. The successful candidate must hold a PhD, have substantial professorial-level research experience with significant publications, and demonstrate a track record of securing major external grants and recognized research excellence. Key responsibilities include mentoring junior researchers and faculty, initiating collaborations with local and international institutions, and advising on academic program quality and staff performance. The position demands strong management and interpersonal skills alongside an established network in industry and education sectors.

title: Assistant Professors/ Senior Lecturers/ Lecturers
department: S.K. Yee School of Health Sciences
description: The position involves teaching and research in pharmaceutical dispensing within health sciences at the lecturer, senior lecturer, or assistant professor level. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in a relevant discipline, with strong preference for those possessing a Doctoral degree and prior experience teaching in tertiary programmes. Fluency in both English and Chinese is essential. The role emphasises expertise in pharmaceutical dispensing and commitment to academic development.

title: [Full-time/ Part-time] Assistant Professor/ Lecturer
department: Yam Pak Charitable Foundation School of Computing and Information Sciences
description: This position requires a computing and information sciences academic to deliver teaching across undergraduate and postgraduate programmes while contributing to curriculum development and review. The role demands a strong research focus, with expectation of publications in top-tier journals, alongside engagement in consultancy and scholarly activities. Additional academic and administrative responsibilities may be assigned by the Dean as needed.

title: Fieldwork Coordinator cum Lecturer/ Senior Lecturer
department: Felizberta Lo Padilla Tong School of Social Sciences
description: The appointee will be responsible (i) to co-ordinate fieldwork placement; (ii) to liaise with agency for fieldwork places; (iii) to convene fieldwork co-ordination other related meetings; and (iv) to perform teaching and related duties.

title: [Full-time/Part-time] Assistant Professors / Senior Lecturers / Lecturers
department: Felizberta Lo Padilla Tong School of Social Sciences
description: This position invites applications for academic roles (Assistant Professor, Senior Lecturer, or Lecturer level) within a social sciences school. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in a relevant discipline and demonstrate teaching or professional experience, though priority will be given to those with doctoral qualifications and evidence of teaching at degree level or strong research records. Professional registration in Hong Kong or internationally is a mandatory requirement for the specific discipline.

title: [Full-time/Part-time] Professors / Associate Professors
department: Felizberta Lo Padilla Tong School of Social Sciences
description: The position involves teaching responsibilities across degree and sub-degree level programmes within the School of Social Sciences. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in a relevant discipline and demonstrate substantial teaching or professional experience, with preference given to those with degree-level teaching experience or a strong research record. Professional registration in Hong Kong or an equivalent overseas jurisdiction is a mandatory requirement for the specific discipline. This is a full-time or part-time appointment at either Professor or Associate Professor level.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
description: This part-time research assistant role supports a humanities research project with responsibilities spanning administrative coordination, data collection and preprocessing, participant recruitment, data annotation, dataset management, and report preparation. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in a humanities field and demonstrate solid research experience alongside strong communication and teamwork capabilities. Bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English, both written and spoken, is essential, along with competent computer skills. The position values collaborative work ethic and the ability to manage multiple research support tasks efficiently.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position supports a five-year psychosocial study examining service outcomes for students with special educational needs, involving literature review, interview coordination and analysis, data analysis, report compilation, and administrative support. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree or higher in education, applied research, psychology, statistics, or social work, with demonstrated experience in quantitative and/or qualitative research methods. The role requires proficiency in conducting in-depth interviews and analyzing quantitative data, alongside strong independent work capabilities, attention to detail, and analytical thinking. Fluency in English and Chinese, combined with excellent communication skills, is essential.

title: Senior Research Associate
department: School of Computing and Information Sciences
description: This role is a Senior Research Associate within a construction technology project focused on monitoring and controlling tower cranes through Digital Twin AI and IoT systems. Key responsibilities include conducting interviews and focus groups, collecting and analyzing quantitative data, and developing operational manuals and training materials. Candidates should hold at least a Bachelor's degree (Master's/MPhil preferred) in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related fields, with demonstrated experience in statistical data analysis, strong independent working ability, and excellent communication and writing skills; prior publication experience is advantageous.

title: Research Assistant
department: S School of Computing and Information Sciences
description: This Research Assistant position involves collecting and analyzing data to evaluate wet electrostatic precipitator performance and recommend improvements, while contributing to peer-reviewed publications and conference presentations. The role requires close collaboration with a project lead and team members to achieve research objectives, along with mentoring responsibilities for undergraduate and graduate students. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field, with demonstrated experience in statistical data analysis, independent work capability, and strong communication and writing skills. Prior publication experience is preferred.

title: Part-time Senior Research Assistant
department: Ip Ying To Lee Yu Yee School of Humanities and Languages
description: This part-time senior research assistant position involves comprehensive support for a research project, including data collection and preprocessing, statistical analysis, trend projection, data management, and report preparation. Candidates must hold a Master's degree, preferably interdisciplinary, with demonstrated research experience and a publication record. The role requires strong communication skills, proficiency in English and computer applications, and flexibility to work across diverse project materials.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
department: Rita Tong Liu School of Business and Hospitality Management
description: Three part-time research assistants will be appointed across distinct disciplinary areas—Accounting & Finance, Entrepreneurship & Management, and Hotel & Tourism & Marketing—each supporting research projects through literature reviews, data collection and analysis, database management, and report preparation. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher with demonstrated research experience and proficiency in statistical and database tools such as SPSS, SQL, and MS Access. Strong communication skills, bilingual competency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua), and solid computer literacy are essential, alongside a collaborative attitude and positive work approach.
